\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Jane Fonda [(info@janepac.com)](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111318190/556828357/-1420927205?refcode=ea.2a34b29c-7a5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.2a34b29c-7a5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhMzRiMjljLTdhNWUtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=dBM9-wHjicfppS0C_cSoWvLEwogS8eL62v3DTQOEVOI=&emci=edb0362e-a85d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2a34b29c-7a5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4823105)
Date: Jul 5, 2025
Subject: Black lung is back
> Donald Trump promised his supporters that reviving the coal industry would bring them prosperity. Instead, _he's sentencing miners to death for profit._
> Black lung disease is making a deadly comeback in America’s coal mines. Modern miners are getting diagnosed in their 40s instead of their 60s, at rates we haven't seen in 50 years. **One in ten working miners nationwide is now estimated to have black lung.**
> It’s easy to point the finger and say this is “what coal country voted for.” But the truth is, Donald Trump made big promises to voters, and now, we’re watching him fail the very communities that put their trust in him.
> Did you know his administration slashed hundreds of jobs at the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health — the agency where scientists and researchers are working to end black lung?
> As a cancer survivor, this is personal to me. I had access to incredible doctors and effective treatments made possible by decades of medical research. I don’t take that for granted. Not for a second. But Trump is attacking the very science that saves lives.
> So this isn’t just about coal country. This is about _all_ of us.
> Coal is one of the biggest drivers of climate change, releasing more carbon dioxide than any other fossil fuel. It puts entire communities at risk of asthma, heart disease, and cancer. And Trump’s coal revival is accelerating a public health crisis that touches every corner of this country.
> **Just look at what he’s done in the past few months alone:**
> * Trump **hijacked emergency powers** to force dirty coal plants to stay open even when they were scheduled to shut down.
> * He’s **gutted clean air** rules that protect communities from mercury, arsenic, and other deadly chemicals.
> * He’s **fast-tracked coal mining on public lands** while cutting environmental protections.
> * He’s **attacked states like California and New Mexico** for trying to transition to clean energy.
> * And he’s just tucked a **massive tax break to coal companies** into his so-called “Big Beautiful Bill.”
>

> **[This is Donald Trump’s America: sacrifice workers and poison communities so fossil fuel billionaires can get richer. But we refuse to back down. My team is gathering signatures from our most active supporters in Cresco and around the country to prove that Trump’s toxic coal agenda is overwhelmingly unpopular. Will you sign the petition to demand Congress step up and stop Trump’s toxic coal agenda?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111318191/556828358/-1420927205?refcode=ea.2a34b29c-7a5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.2a34b29c-7a5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJhMzRiMjljLTdhNWUtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=dBM9-wHjicfppS0C_cSoWvLEwogS8eL62v3DTQOEVOI=&emci=edb0362e-a85d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2a34b29c-7a5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4823105)**

clean_body: We’re still missing your signature. | Did you see Jane Fonda’s message? She sounded the alarm about Trump’s dangerous push to revive dirty, polluting coal at the expense of our health, our safety, and our communities. Trump's newly signed spending bill forces at least 4 million acres of our public lands to open up for coal mining and incentivizes coal companies to poison our communities with new tax handouts and lower government fees. All of this is happening despite the fact that renewable energy is cheaper, cleaner, and more reliable. But Trump doesn’t care about facts. He cares about billionaires and corporations who profit from pollution. We're preparing to send our petition to Congress soon, and over 2,500 grassroots supporters all across the country have already signed on to reject Trump's coal agenda and demand that Washington lawmakers step in and take action. | No matter how hard Trump and Big Oil try to silence us, we will rise stronger and more united than ever. Team JanePAC Begin forwarded message: \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Jane Fonda Date: Jul 5, 2025 Subject: Black lung is back Donald Trump promised his supporters that reviving the coal industry would bring them prosperity. Instead, he's sentencing miners to death for profit. Black lung disease is making a deadly comeback in America’s coal mines. Modern miners are getting diagnosed in their 40s instead of their 60s, at rates we haven't seen in 50 years. One in ten working miners nationwide is now estimated to have black lung. It’s easy to point the finger and say this is “what coal country voted for.” But the truth is, Donald Trump made big promises to voters, and now, we’re watching him fail the very communities that put their trust in him. Did you know his administration slashed hundreds of jobs at the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health — the agency where scientists and researchers are working to end black lung? As a cancer survivor, this is personal to me. I had access to incredible doctors and effective treatments made possible by decades of medical research. I don’t take that for granted. Not for a second. But Trump is attacking the very science that saves lives. So this isn’t just about coal country. This is about all of us. Coal is one of the biggest drivers of climate change, releasing more carbon dioxide than any other fossil fuel. It puts entire communities at risk of asthma, heart disease, and cancer. And Trump’s coal revival is accelerating a public health crisis that touches every corner of this country. Just look at what he’s done in the past few months alone: Trump hijacked emergency powers to force dirty coal plants to stay open even when they were scheduled to shut down. He’s gutted clean air rules that protect communities from mercury, arsenic, and other deadly chemicals. He’s fast-tracked coal mining on public lands while cutting environmental protections. He’s attacked states like California and New Mexico for trying to transition to clean energy. And he’s just tucked a massive tax break to coal companies into his so-called “Big Beautiful Bill.” --- My team will be sending signatures to Congress next week, so sign on now to make your voice heard. Let’s keep the pressure going. – Jane Fonda | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

name: Nancy Boyd
subject: Investing in the next generation of leaders.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten for Congress
body:
We’re investing in the next generation of leaders because building a better future means empowering young people to lead the way.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111318306/556828674/32658279?refcode=BLU_EM_HSC_2025-07-11_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMmIyMDY3ZC03YjVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5yxwoTyAAjFrkb3IeptzOyQBfH5Z8OdtVHOI-uHciw4=)
---
| Hi team,

It’s Nancy Boyd, Hillary’s Political Advisor. I'm excited to share some news with you: **Democracy Summer is back!**
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111318307/556828675/32658279?refcode=BLU_EM_HSC_2025-07-11_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMmIyMDY3ZC03YjVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5yxwoTyAAjFrkb3IeptzOyQBfH5Z8OdtVHOI-uHciw4=)
As a former Democracy Summer fellow myself, I know just how valuable an experience like this can be. From canvassing to fundraising and voter engagement, campaign fellows will receive hands-on training that builds real political skills.

This program is more than just a summer job. It’s an opportunity to learn from some of the top organizers and political leaders in the country while making a lasting impact right here in Michigan’s 3rd District.

We’re investing in the next generation of leaders because building a better future means empowering young people to lead the way.

This work would not be possible without supporters like you.

With gratitude,

Nancy Boyd
| | Hillary Scholten is a mom, attorney, and fourth-generation West Michigander representing MI-03 in the House of Representatives. My wife is Winsome Earle-Sears. If you haven't met her yet, she is a conservative Christian running for Governor against a George Soros-backed liberal. That's the power of my wife's positive message that she's spreading!
Winsome's dad immigrated to America with $1.75 in his pocket and brought his family over, including a young Winsome, once he established himself. They were able to make ends meet here, and live the American Dream they came to achieve.
Because she was black, she spent a lot of her life thinking she was a Democrat. But when George Bush, Sr. was running for president, she heard him say that if all you ever have is welfare, you will never have anything to pass on to your children.
Now, she is a leading conservative voice, spreading her message about the American Dream and the opportunities created by the Republican Party.
Listen, Winsome has won with her positive message & no-quit attitude before. I see her fight every day and I know she can do it again. Her Democrat opponent has absurd amounts of money with more pouring in from George Soros-backed groups and Hollywood Leftists every day. I can promise you this: Winsome needs you in her corner to win this crucial race! [Today, I’m personally asking you to step up with a donation of $5, $10, or even $25 to support the love of my life in the most important election of 2025. clean_body: Peter, I'm Terence Sears. My wife is Winsome Earle-Sears. If you haven't met her yet, she is a conservative Christian running for Governor against a George Soros-backed liberal. Winsome's opponent has millions in dark money, but the race is still TIED. That's the power of my wife's positive message that she's spreading! Winsome's dad immigrated to America with $1.75 in his pocket and brought his family over, including a young Winsome, once he established himself. They were able to make ends meet here, and live the American Dream they came to achieve. Because she was black, she spent a lot of her life thinking she was a Democrat. But when George Bush, Sr. was running for president, she heard him say that if all you ever have is welfare, you will never have anything to pass on to your children. Now, she is a leading conservative voice, spreading her message about the American Dream and the opportunities created by the Republican Party. Listen, Winsome has won with her positive message & no-quit attitude before. I see her fight every day and I know she can do it again. Her Democrat opponent has absurd amounts of money with more pouring in from George Soros-backed groups and Hollywood Leftists every day. I can promise you this: Winsome needs you in her corner to win this crucial race! Every penny you send in today will go toward Winsome's fight. It'll be spent on events, TV ads, getting out the vote, and campaign signs. I can't understate how critical all of these things are in our fight. I hope you'll chip in today and ensure the best wife and mother a guy could ever ask for becomes Virginia's next Governor! Thanks so much and may God bless you! -Terence Sears | | | | | | Paid for by Winsome for Governor Military disclaimer: Neither military info nor photographs of Winsome Earle-Sears in uniform imply endorsement of Winsome Earle-Sears by the Department of Defense or its particular military departments. As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account are not tax deductible for federal income tax purposes.

clean_body: Fellow Conservative, We need to have an honest conversation about who we allow into our country. Let me introduce you to Zohran Mamdani: The socialist candidate for mayor of New York City. It took this guy approximately ZERO days to confirm our worst suspicions. Mamdani, a self-declared socialist, wants to "globalize the intifada" and import values into the West that will destabilize our civilization. Everything about him runs counter to America First. The fact that he could win in arguably America 's greatest city proves that, while we won Congress and the White House, the fight for the heart, soul, and future of America still rages on. Turning Point PAC is working harder than ever to . We must ensure that radical socialists like Mamdani do NOT gain power in cities, counties, and states across the country. Help us and by donating today. Fellow Conservative, the future of Trump 's agenda will be decided on the streets of Los Angeles, Chicago, and New York. While I frequently run into those who think that conservatives should just throw up their hands and leave New York to its fate, it's a mistake. New York symbolizes America to the world. It's the nerve center for the entire global economy. It has America's most famous sports teams, museums, buildings, and more. It is the global capital of finance, media, and the arts. America simply giving up on New York is like the British Empire giving up on London or the Roman Empire giving up on Rome. And of course, that 's exactly why Marxists like Mamdani want to conquer it. If we won't fight for one of our nation's crown jewels, then we deserve to lose it. Turning Point PAC is taking back critical swing states like Arizona, Nevada, and New Hampshire from radical socialists like Mamdani. But we can't do it without you, Fellow Conservative. Please donate today to make America First the law of the land in 2026, 2028, and beyond. body:
Last week, Donald Trump signed into law his trash budget bill that will strip 14 MILLION Americans of healthcare and put millions of people at risk of losing their SNAP benefits.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111319636/556832850/-629675555?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWZkNTQ3NjUtN2U1ZS1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ay9IcUrIBifB-trStYx3XaWe9WZY_wHa5l2n8dd_2nE=) **Last week, Donald Trump signed into law his trash budget bill that will strip 14 MILLION Americans of healthcare and put millions of people at risk of losing their SNAP benefits.** _And it’s all to pay for tax handouts for the richest people who have ever lived._ This will cause irreparable harm to Oregonians and people across the country. That’s why I voted against it — along with every single one of my Democratic colleagues in Washington. And with a majority of voters also OPPOSING this trash bill, it’s clear that our fight is far from over. Right now, it’s critical that we continue to stand up to Trump and his Republican allies and fight for real change — like lowering the cost of living and working to make life easier for working families. **The best way we can keep up that fight is by ensuring Democrats have what it takes to flip the House blue next year — and that starts with defending the most vulnerable Democratic-held seats like this one.** **[Please, if you want to stand up to Trump and help put an end to his extreme agenda, will you chip in now and support our fight to defend OR-05 and flip the House? clean_body: Last week, Donald Trump signed into law his trash budget bill that will strip 14 MILLION Americans of healthcare and put millions of people at risk of losing their SNAP benefits. = Last week, Donald Trump signed into law his trash budget bill that will strip 14 MILLION Americans of healthcare and put millions of people at risk of losing their SNAP benefits. And it’s all to pay for tax handouts for the richest people who have ever lived. This will cause irreparable harm to Oregonians and people across the country. That’s why I voted against it — along with every single one of my Democratic colleagues in Washington. And with a majority of voters also OPPOSING this trash bill, it’s clear that our fight is far from over. Right now, it’s critical that we continue to stand up to Trump and his Republican allies and fight for real change — like lowering the cost of living and working to make life easier for working families. The best way we can keep up that fight is by ensuring Democrats have what it takes to flip the House blue next year — and that starts with defending the most vulnerable Democratic-held seats like this one. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Thank you, Janelle | Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. Republican cuts to FEMA's budget have dismantled critical services that many Americans, especially those with disabilities, rely on during times of disaster. The decision to end FEMA’s essential door-to-door assistance program is a grave betrayal to the disabled community. This program was a lifeline, providing crucial support to those who are often the most vulnerable in emergencies. **Now, without these vital boots on the ground, countless individuals will face increased risks and hardships during disasters.** [**To counter this, Able Dems is launching an ad campaign to hold Republicans accountable. clean_body: | | | ** **** Republican cuts to FEMA's budget have dismantled critical services that many Americans, especially those with disabilities, rely on during times of disaster. The decision to end FEMA’s essential door-to-door assistance program is a grave betrayal to the disabled community. This program was a lifeline, providing crucial support to those who are often the most vulnerable in emergencies. Now, without these vital boots on the ground, countless individuals will face increased risks and hardships during disasters. **If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. The Republican agenda continues to strip away essential programs, ignoring the needs of communities that depend on them the most. These shortsighted policies are dismantling the very foundations of our safety nets, leaving millions at risk. Losing FEMA’s door-to-door assistance means the disabled community won’t get this life-saving service!** Join us in this fight for justice and equity. Thank you for your support. -Able Dems | elects Democrats with disabilities. We fight to protect accessible voting, expand health care and Social Security, and equality for all. To donate by check, send to: Able Dems PAC c/o Pocketbook Strategies 1110 N Virgil Ave #375 Los Angeles, CA 90029 \- \- Paid for by Able Dems PAC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to Able Dems PAC are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Sent via . In the most expensive primary in New York City history, we defeated a political dynasty, billionaires, and the conservative machine behind them — all powered by people like you. **They said it was impossible. But with thousands of volunteers, small-dollar donors, and the Working Families Party by our side, we proved that organizing beats money — every time.** Now, we face the next challenge: the general election in November. [**To show our strength, we need to make a powerful statement by our first general election fundraising deadline tonight, July 11th, at midnight ET. name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: I was on MSNBC last week
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=496%2E181023%2ETiKjO2)
Team, I want to be honest with you: Some days, the challenges in this country feel so big, it’s hard to know where to start.
People are sick and tired of politics, frustrated with leadership that doesn’t listen to them, and frankly, feel powerless in the face of all the problems.
**But we aren’t powerless. We hold the power.**
The citizens of our democracy, the American citizens, are the most powerful people in this moment. Even though so much of the noise and the chaos is designed to make us feel small, _**we are the ones with the power to move us forward.** _**Here’s how:**
We need to be citizens engaging, not just voting, but willing to be a poll worker or volunteer for the process. That’s how we find our way through this dark moment into a time where our democracy remains strong, healthy, and robust.
**If the citizens demand that democracy works, it will work.**
And the power of the people will always be greater than the people in power — when we choose to exercise that power.
So, for those feeling discouraged or wanting to check out, know that that's exactly what those who are against democracy want us to do.
**But if we dig in, sign up, volunteer, register to vote, be a poll worker, vote, and encourage others to vote, then the forces that are trying to silence us cannot win because our voices in our democracy will always rule the day.**
That’s the message I shared on _MSNBC_ last week, and I hope you take these words to heart, too. **[And if this message speaks to you, please pitch in to my campaign to help me share this message far and wide across Michigan.](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q32025.OcO0Lx&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250711_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=496%2E181023%2ETiKjO2)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Jocelyn

name: =?utf-8?B?4oCLSmFtZXMgVXRobWVpZXI=?=
subject: ​I have a message for the radical left
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj205fv7r1cwxvc2f0qmelezkif43vr5wn2tzb0aqxjt8ffbkwe6f62yx98ve8lfk1jfxeg0gfjuttgok7afy&__stmp=sz8y4g&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz8y4g&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol00z49ptm0xksprl598lh5rf33m4k7mqq07u62kbvmlme08asnzlyf96a4ajabvuiwc315ractj2qb9u94sg9jjy87mr2e98qwxethi9iqyr9h0wn60xeyjisl8fzubf1kta30ak99v&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz8y4g&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz8y4g&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz8y4g&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz8y4g&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol00z49ptm0xksprl598lh5rf33m4k7mqq07u62kbvmlme08asnzlyf96a4ajabvuiwc315ractj2qb9u94sg9jjy87mr2e98qwxethi9iqyr9h0wn60xeyjisl8fzubf1kta30ak99v&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz8y4g&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz8y4g&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Pure evil
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Trump signed his monstrous budget bill into law, and there’s only one way
to describe it: pure evil.

This bill slashes hundreds of billions of dollars from Medicaid and food
assistance, adds $75 billion in funding to ICE, rolls back investments in
stopping climate change, and gives huge tax breaks to the richest
Americans.

Make no mistake: this bill will kill people and endanger our neighbors.

If you're a family, senior, worker, parent, child, farmer, immigrant,
working class, middle class, disabled, unemployed, sick, hungry,
Republican, Democrat, Independent, this bill was written to harm you.

And if you're a billionaire, this bill is great for you, and you only.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250711?refcode=em20250711-36835&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36835_1203867_iB5fmf&akid=36835%2E1203867%2EiB5fmf ]Donate to Justice Democrats to help us vote out the people responsible
for this bill >>

It's despicable that 220 Republicans spend their time in D.C. scheming to
steal healthcare, food, and basic necessities from millions so they can
fund ICE gestapo and trillion dollar tax cuts for their richest
billionaire donors.

They have only one value—greed—and they must be voted out in mass.

That’s exactly what Justice Democrats is working towards in the 2026
midterm elections. We endorse unapologetic progressives who will take on
not just the GOP, but the do-nothing Democrats who enable them with
silence and inaction.

And those are exactly the types of candidates we need across the country
if we're going to inspire voters to believe in this Party enough to take
back the majority from Donald Trump and Mike Johnson.

Congress doesn’t have to serve the rich and powerful. Programs like
Medicaid, Medicare, and Social Security were once seen as revolutionary
ideas, until the people organized and made them a reality.

We don’t just need defenders of the status quo. We need candidates
fighting for a bold vision for our future—Medicare for All, a Green New
Deal, a living wage, and abolishing ICE. That’s how we’ll win the
working-class power needed to make our government work for everybody.

Those are the types of fighters who are Justice Democrats. We’ve already
endorsed our first new candidate of the cycle—Donavan McKinney in
Michigan’s 13th district—and more endorsements are on the way. But we need
your help to elect them.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250711?refcode=em20250711-36835&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36835_1203867_iB5fmf&akid=36835%2E1203867%2EiB5fmf ]Can you donate $5 to help us hold Congress accountable
for passing this disastrous budget? Our representatives need to know there
are consequences for voting for massive handouts for the rich that gut
programs for the working class.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250711?refcode=em20250711-36835&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36835_1203867_iB5fmf&akid=36835%2E1203867%2EiB5fmf ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250711-36835-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36835_1203867_iB5fmf&akid=36835%2E1203867%2EiB5fmf

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36835%2E1203867%2EiB5fmf

Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com
clean_body: [1]Justice Democrats Peter, Trump signed his monstrous budget bill into law, and there’s only one way to describe it: pure evil. This bill slashes hundreds of billions of dollars from Medicaid and food assistance, adds $75 billion in funding to ICE, rolls back investments in stopping climate change, and gives huge tax breaks to the richest Americans. Make no mistake: this bill will kill people and endanger our neighbors. If you're a family, senior, worker, parent, child, farmer, immigrant, working class, middle class, disabled, unemployed, sick, hungry, Republican, Democrat, Independent, this bill was written to harm you. And if you're a billionaire, this bill is great for you, and you only. [ ]Donate to Justice Democrats to help us vote out the people responsible for this bill >> It's despicable that 220 Republicans spend their time in D.C. scheming to steal healthcare, food, and basic necessities from millions so they can fund ICE gestapo and trillion dollar tax cuts for their richest billionaire donors. They have only one value—greed—and they must be voted out in mass. That’s exactly what Justice Democrats is working towards in the 2026 midterm elections. We endorse unapologetic progressives who will take on not just the GOP, but the do-nothing Democrats who enable them with silence and inaction. And those are exactly the types of candidates we need across the country if we're going to inspire voters to believe in this Party enough to take back the majority from Donald Trump and Mike Johnson. Congress doesn’t have to serve the rich and powerful. Programs like Medicaid, Medicare, and Social Security were once seen as revolutionary ideas, until the people organized and made them a reality. We don’t just need defenders of the status quo. We need candidates fighting for a bold vision for our future—Medicare for All, a Green New Deal, a living wage, and abolishing ICE. That’s how we’ll win the working-class power needed to make our government work for everybody. Those are the types of fighters who are Justice Democrats. We’ve already endorsed our first new candidate of the cycle—Donavan McKinney in Michigan’s 13th district—and more endorsements are on the way. But we need your help to elect them. [ ]Can you donate $5 to help us hold Congress accountable for passing this disastrous budget? Our representatives need to know there are consequences for voting for massive handouts for the rich that gut programs for the working class. “Trump administration withholds $70 million in K-12 school funding from Colorado”
— Denver Post, 7/2/25
The Trump administration is blocking critical school funding from Colorado kids. Jason is demanding answers.
Join him by adding your name to defend our public schools.

Team,

Colorado’s kids deserve better. Right now, the Trump administration is holding $70 million in federal education funding from CO public schools with no clear explanation.

That’s money that should be going to classrooms, teachers, and students who rely on it.

Jason knows our children’s futures should never be used as a political bargaining chip for partisan fights. That’s why he’s joining fellow Colorado Democrats to demand answers and fight to get this funding restored immediately.

We need transparency and accountability from the Department of Education — and our public schools deserve every dollar owed to them.

Add your name today to stand with Jason Crow in demanding answers, defending our kids’ education, and holding the Trump administration accountable. [https://act.jasoncrowforcongress.com/a/2025-07-defend-schools]

name: Shahid Buttar
subject: The audacity of ignorance
body:
Climate denialism invites death & destruction, as institutional ignorance invites more preventable risks in the future

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..629g8fUlvZhzzez0Vu7nRQrLeGV4Q05KkJ5DqzMjKF8?) for more
---
[](https://substack..kjbAzkSSlBVGaPdULjg0S0cmgo5PDvIbuFQafTIHgug?)
---
# [The audacity of ignorance](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI1OTE4OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTE4Mzg5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7AmNWx-E3Yk4d_HR2r7ewTwIg18ZYPp_lHXzt6caUSs)
### Climate denialism invites death & destruction, as institutional ignorance invites more preventable risks in the future
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Jul 15
---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjU5MTg5OCwiZXhwIjoxNzU1MTgzODk4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.f2yt3rhtxSjos2cLlL9O4M06qk2mvmVa4DBTlKCtJIk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI1OTE4OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTE4Mzg5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7AmNWx-E3Yk4d_HR2r7ewTwIg18ZYPp_lHXzt6caUSs&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI1OTE4OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTE4Mzg5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7AmNWx-E3Yk4d_HR2r7ewTwIg18ZYPp_lHXzt6caUSs)
---
| | [](https://substack..7lY402LqHpBEI5TZYo-Qkdc7OsQTe1cUdknNFdKgm-Y?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/the-audacity-of-ignorance?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Over a hundred [Texans are dead](https://substack.com/redirect/278b48c4-f729-44c4-b24c-41ab50267f23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and more remain missing--due to the predicted impacts of stupid decisions that experts warned would lead to catastrophe.
They will not be the last Americans to die of preventable causes. They, and too many who will follow in their tragic footsteps, are victims of the plague of institutional ignorance that has befallen this once-great country.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0ec32a95-9416-4d95-b593-0de8550a24be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The recent floods in Texas not only revealed the [administrative devastation](https://substack.com/redirect/aac3a680-f6ff-4400-a404-943fe7fdd7bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) wrought by Trump & Musk during their short time together in Washington, but also foreshadow the equally--likely more--dangerous ignorance of science more broadly, reflected in cuts and interventions at public agencies that used to support science, such as the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and National Science Foundation (NSF).
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI1OTE4OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTE4Mzg5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7AmNWx-E3Yk4d_HR2r7ewTwIg18ZYPp_lHXzt6caUSs)
### **Musk has the blood of over 100 Texans on his hands**
Would over 100 people have died in last week's floods in Texas if federal weather monitoring agencies were adequately staffed?
Corporate journalists have bent over backwards to indulge supposed [questions](https://substack.com/redirect/95787c5b-0c93-408d-9f7b-dc7f72116530?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the cause of the floods, as if their resolution were not already brutally established by dozens of dead bodies in the wake of [clear warnings](https://substack.com/redirect/3e20def6-b329-447c-ad40-661f62f3dba0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) before the [staff cuts driven by Trump & Musk](https://substack.com/redirect/76579254-6def-4470-8057-098d6743fc86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that eviscerated the National Weather Service.
On May 2--two months before the floods--a [joint letter](https://substack.com/redirect/1cf9acc0-8469-4758-8f48-aad50a446522?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) signed by _every_ former director of the National Weather Service issued a stark warning. In no uncertain terms, they shared "our worst nightmare...that weather forecast offices will be so understaffed that there will be needless loss of life."
Nor was their warning obscure. Reports about it were published by outlets including [NBC News](https://substack.com/redirect/3e20def6-b329-447c-ad40-661f62f3dba0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which noted that:
> The former directors -- who served between 1988 and 2022 -- said that between the Trump administration's cuts to probationary workers and personnel reductions through buyouts, the weather service's staffing has been reduced by more than 10% during the busiest time for severe storm predictions.
That was demonstrably the case in Texas last week.
_If you 've learned something from Chronicles of a Dying Empire, don't stop now! Subscriptions are free, although paid subscriptions help expand my work._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..8u36ZUkMhRk8MSRh5e0lIMMVXG_0lgNSVTPC0Y4Rjpc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168094097)
### **We reap what they sow**
The decision to hamstring the federal agencies responsible for weather forecasting did not simply fall from the sky. It was a legislative decision with clear and identifiable sponsors, including U.S. Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX).
Before the so-called "Big Beautiful" reconciliation bill was signed into law by the President--on the very same day that the floods began to devastate the Texas hill country--[Cruz inserted language](https://substack.com/redirect/57666a8e-3e53-44a1-9d78-de33815b1376?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that eliminated a $150 million fund to "accelerate advances and improvements in [weather forecasting] research, observation systems, modeling, forecasting, assessments, and dissemination of information to the public."
Sen. Cruz also cut a separate $50 million in grants that had previously allowed the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to study climate-related impacts on oceans, weather systems and coastal ecosystems.
Decisions have consequences.
And accountability matters.
Flood survivor Kathy Perkins was interviewed at a Red Cross center in Kerrville, TX last Sunday after the floods damaged her trailer home. Her comments captured some of the understandable outrage felt by people who narrowly escaped with their lives. She said that "Many people are angry right now," and that " elected officials "won't just be able to go hush-hush," because "[[t]hose kids should have been safe and they weren't](https://substack.com/redirect/652cd9c2-80df-40db-984b-da34c574cc3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
Hunt, TX resident Lesa Baird, who was also staying at the same Red Cross shelter, confirmed that she heard "no warning at all, none whatsoever" before the floods washed away her home.
Policymakers whose choices force communities to endure preventable death & destruction have no business serving in office.
Of course, if our political system allowed any modicum of accountability, most of Congress would be out of a job. Yet despite widespread [condemnation](https://substack.com/redirect/6683f429-323a-4e46-b86e-e38f4c718490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Congress, the re-election rate among its Members remains [well above 90%](https://substack.com/redirect/a482f185-cf94-4700-9043-117cd1a115a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **More to come**
Beyond the cuts driven by Congress and the president to staffing at weather services, similarly manufactured crises at [NIH](https://substack.com/redirect/894af4a8-f0af-4b0d-87a2-40ecb8da6949?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [NSF](https://substack.com/redirect/63eb268a-c8dc-4a42-808f-315aca0402cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) threaten scientific awareness of any number of emerging threats. The future of the U.S. is one blinded to its risks, from discrete reflections of the climate crisis to its broader contours, from threats to agriculture and food security to threats to water supplies and the security of basic needs.
Even before reeling from the floods, Texas was already the epicenter of the worst measles outbreak in 30 years. The World Health Organization announced the [eradication of measles in the U.S. 25 years ago](https://substack.com/redirect/1afec245-de89-47fc-b99c-081d57d77c1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but an ongoing outbreak centered in Texas that started this year has sparked [over a thousand cases](https://substack.com/redirect/53de6097-8df6-4d50-9e7c-1e02246837df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Over 155 people have gone to the hospital, and at least three of them have died. Most reported cases are involving children.
Every one of these measles cases represents a failure of both policy and practice. As one fellow writer on this platform has noted, "Our country's descent into an [unnecessary measles pandemic is a new level of maddening](https://open.substack.com/pub/discourseblog/p/what-the-fuck-are-we-doing?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios)."
David Edmonston was 11 years old when, in 1954, doctors in Boston [collected samples](https://substack.com/redirect/ed028845-0cdf-4d34-ae85-4e975c285d43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from his throat and his blood. Their work eventually enabled the development of the world's first measles vaccine, and helped win the [Nobel Prize](https://substack.com/redirect/ce41a124-074b-4c2b-b90e-52b6049ca76f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Physiology or Medicine for Harvard professor John Franklin Enders.
Before the development of the measles vaccine [in 1963](https://substack.com/redirect/5bdfe12a-1454-4666-9390-1801302c1433?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the disease was both highly contagious and endemic across the United States. Each year, approximately 4 million Americans fell ill, and nearly 50,000 required hospitalization. Measles deaths in the U.S. [declined](https://substack.com/redirect/ed028845-0cdf-4d34-ae85-4e975c285d43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from 500 per year to none.
Today, Edmonston--whose blood helped create the measles vaccine--is 82 years old. Even though his attitudes towards vaccines have shifted over time and grew complicated during the COVID outbreak, he [said recently](https://substack.com/redirect/ed028845-0cdf-4d34-ae85-4e975c285d43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "vaccines are very important not just for the individuals who take them, but for our society as a whole."
Referencing federal regulators, Edmonston noted that "There's an awful lot of misinformation out there, and you need to be careful who you listen to."
As absurd as deaths from measles in 2025 may appear, they remain a signal of more to come.
According to Lauren Gardner, leader of Johns Hopkins University's databases tracking disease outbreaks, "What we're seeing with measles is a little bit of a ['canary in a coal mine](https://substack.com/redirect/53de6097-8df6-4d50-9e7c-1e02246837df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's indicative of a problem that we know exists with vaccination attitudes in this county and...[is] likely to get worse."
_Less than 1% of subscribers to this newsletter actively support its expansion. Please join us to enable me to write more frequently!_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..8u36ZUkMhRk8MSRh5e0lIMMVXG_0lgNSVTPC0Y4Rjpc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168094097)
### **Prioritizing politics rather than survival**
Why have Texans been reduced to cannon fodder for the dumbest domestic decisions of the empire?
Even though the state is reeling from multiple preventable disasters, its elected leaders appear more focused on their own interests than those of their constituents. Within days of the floods, Texas Governor Greg Abbott published his [list of stated priorities](https://substack.com/redirect/475806b3-1c08-417b-9e9f-25377005f29c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the coming year.
While several items on the list related to building better early warning systems for "flood prone areas throughout Texas," his list also included a focus on [contriving a larger GOP majority in the U.S. House](https://substack.com/redirect/f0f02b17-3837-4bd2-af6e-2a253092210d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Representatives by gerrymandering the state even more than it already has been.
Last year, the Brennan Center for Justice at NYU [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/4dba484f-2cf1-4892-9947-80ef0ebc2736?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "When Texas Republicans redrew their state's congressional map [in 2024], they took what had been one of the most competitive and politically interesting maps in the country and, through discrimination against fast-growing racial and ethnic minorities, transformed it into one of the most boring and least dynamic." The Department of Justice sued the State of Texas the same week that report was released, alleging that the state legislature's gerrymander is intentionally racially discriminatory and violates the Voting Rights Act.
While his constituents are dying by the dozens from floods, and flooding hospitals due to a disease outbreak, Abbott's greatest concerns include building his party's majority by further degrading democracy within his state.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI1OTE4OTgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTE4Mzg5OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7AmNWx-E3Yk4d_HR2r7ewTwIg18ZYPp_lHXzt6caUSs)
### **America 's future**
The unfolding debacles across Texas should expose the true nature of America's future intended by the Trump administration. We'll have plenty of ICE agents but not enough scientists, plenty of police but not enough medicine, plenty of weapons but not enough food.
Welcome to the future of America, in all its ugly and sordid glory.
This is an era of institutional ignorance, with all the surprises that we could expect from electing leaders who bury their heads in the sand.
It is difficult to convey the idiocy of the past week's events. There is not a single reason to dismiss the floods as an unpreventable calamity when the professionals who used to issue warnings made clear when they were fired that the system they staffed would suffer from inadequate staffing.
Nor does explaining reality constitute politicizing a disaster. It is simply a matter of connecting cause and effect--just like scientists (for whom the administration and its right wing enablers harbor such disdain) do every day.
_Paid subscribers can access evidence of a major social media platform algorithmically suppressing the availability of content by dissenting voices. This suppression tilts the discursive playing field, violates the vision of user-generated content[promoted](https://substack.com/redirect/a118b20c-d443-41a1-9924-dbbdeff45ccb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by social media companies in the early 2010s, and indicates why readers who share my articles play such a crucial part in the process of informing the public._
I recently received a message from a reader who suggested she was unable to access posts pointing at my articles from Instagram, in particular.
Our exchange was sufficiently alarming that I figured I'd share it.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/afe8ce49-2e31-461a-995c-060b121b17f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
One reason I wanted to share this exchange was that it indicates why I rely so much on readers to share my work. We might imagine that, in the age of the Internet, the most thoughtful writing and analysis might reach the most readers.
Reality, however, suggests otherwise.
In addition to the content of our exchange indicating a major social media platform leaning on the discursive scale by inhibiting the visibility of particular perspectives, another aspect of our exchange that I found interesting is the context for it: this person is an immigrant, and understandably feels inhibited from speaking publicly about issues even vaguely relating to politics.
Welcome to America in 2025.
You're currently a free subscriber to [Chronicles of a Dying Empire](https://substack.com/redirect/a3e5d7b0-bebd-4c6d-9b54-73dc1f9abf0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For the full experience, [upgrade your subscription.](https://substack..8u36ZUkMhRk8MSRh5e0lIMMVXG_0lgNSVTPC0Y4Rjpc?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=postcta)
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..8u36ZUkMhRk8MSRh5e0lIMMVXG_0lgNSVTPC0Y4Rjpc?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=postcta)

### Climate denialism invites death & destruction, as institutional ignorance invites more preventable risks in the future

Over a hundred [Texans are dead](https://substack.com/redirect/278b48c4-f729-44c4-b24c-41ab50267f23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and more remain missing--due to the predicted impacts of stupid decisions that experts warned would lead to catastrophe.
They will not be the last Americans to die of preventable causes. They, and too many who will follow in their tragic footsteps, are victims of the plague of institutional ignorance that has befallen this once-great country.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0ec32a95-9416-4d95-b593-0de8550a24be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The recent floods in Texas not only revealed the [administrative devastation](https://substack.com/redirect/aac3a680-f6ff-4400-a404-943fe7fdd7bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) wrought by Trump & Musk during their short time together in Washington, but also foreshadow the equally--likely more--dangerous ignorance of science more broadly, reflected in cuts and interventions at public agencies that used to support science, such as the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and National Science Foundation (NSF).
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIyNTc1NjYsImV4cCI6MTc1NDg0OTU2NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KqRuKbqw3kT7DK0VV4UiSnRvEup_i3K7iX82WILraXA)
### **Musk has the blood of over 100 Texans on his hands**
Would over 100 people have died in last week's floods in Texas if federal weather monitoring agencies were adequately staffed?
Corporate journalists have bent over backwards to indulge supposed [questions](https://substack.com/redirect/95787c5b-0c93-408d-9f7b-dc7f72116530?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the cause of the floods, as if their resolution were not already brutally established by dozens of dead bodies in the wake of [clear warnings](https://substack.com/redirect/3e20def6-b329-447c-ad40-661f62f3dba0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) before the [staff cuts driven by Trump & Musk](https://substack.com/redirect/76579254-6def-4470-8057-098d6743fc86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that eviscerated the National Weather Service.
On May 2--two months before the floods--a [joint letter](https://substack.com/redirect/1cf9acc0-8469-4758-8f48-aad50a446522?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) signed by _every_ former director of the National Weather Service issued a stark warning. In no uncertain terms, they shared "our worst nightmare...that weather forecast offices will be so understaffed that there will be needless loss of life."
Nor was their warning obscure. Reports about it were published by outlets including [NBC News](https://substack.com/redirect/3e20def6-b329-447c-ad40-661f62f3dba0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which noted that:
> The former directors -- who served between 1988 and 2022 -- said that between the Trump administration's cuts to probationary workers and personnel reductions through buyouts, the weather service's staffing has been reduced by more than 10% during the busiest time for severe storm predictions.
That was demonstrably the case in Texas last week.
_If you 've learned something from Chronicles of a Dying Empire, don't stop now! Subscriptions are free, although paid subscriptions help expand my work._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..5MQfQRVtntj7jSoT4tHHaTubFGP2uj1SicXqhAc-YPU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168094097)
### **We reap what they sow**
The decision to hamstring the federal agencies responsible for weather forecasting did not simply fall from the sky. It was a legislative decision with clear and identifiable sponsors, including U.S. Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX).
Before the so-called "Big Beautiful" reconciliation bill was signed into law by the President--on the very same day that the floods began to devastate the Texas hill country--[Cruz inserted language](https://substack.com/redirect/57666a8e-3e53-44a1-9d78-de33815b1376?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that eliminated a $150 million fund to "accelerate advances and improvements in [weather forecasting] research, observation systems, modeling, forecasting, assessments, and dissemination of information to the public."
Sen. Cruz also cut a separate $50 million in grants that had previously allowed the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to study climate-related impacts on oceans, weather systems and coastal ecosystems.
Decisions have consequences.
And accountability matters.
Flood survivor Kathy Perkins was interviewed at a Red Cross center in Kerrville, TX last Sunday after the floods damaged her trailer home. Her comments captured some of the understandable outrage felt by people who narrowly escaped with their lives. She said that "Many people are angry right now," and that " elected officials "won't just be able to go hush-hush," because "[[t]hose kids should have been safe and they weren't](https://substack.com/redirect/652cd9c2-80df-40db-984b-da34c574cc3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
Hunt, TX resident Lesa Baird, who was also staying at the same Red Cross shelter, confirmed that she heard "no warning at all, none whatsoever" before the floods washed away her home.
Policymakers whose choices force communities to endure preventable death & destruction have no business serving in office.
Of course, if our political system allowed any modicum of accountability, most of Congress would be out of a job. Yet despite widespread [condemnation](https://substack.com/redirect/6683f429-323a-4e46-b86e-e38f4c718490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Congress, the re-election rate among its Members remains [well above 90%](https://substack.com/redirect/a482f185-cf94-4700-9043-117cd1a115a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **More to come**
Beyond the cuts driven by Congress and the president to staffing at weather services, similarly manufactured crises at [NIH](https://substack.com/redirect/894af4a8-f0af-4b0d-87a2-40ecb8da6949?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [NSF](https://substack.com/redirect/63eb268a-c8dc-4a42-808f-315aca0402cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) threaten scientific awareness of any number of emerging threats. The future of the U.S. is one blinded to its risks, from discrete reflections of the climate crisis to its broader contours, from threats to agriculture and food security to threats to water supplies and the security of basic needs.
Even before reeling from the floods, Texas was already the epicenter of the worst measles outbreak in 30 years. The World Health Organization announced the [eradication of measles in the U.S. 25 years ago](https://substack.com/redirect/1afec245-de89-47fc-b99c-081d57d77c1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but an ongoing outbreak centered in Texas that started this year has sparked [over a thousand cases](https://substack.com/redirect/53de6097-8df6-4d50-9e7c-1e02246837df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Over 155 people have gone to the hospital, and at least three of them have died. Most reported cases are involving children.
Every one of these measles cases represents a failure of both policy and practice. As one fellow writer on this platform has noted, "Our country's descent into an [unnecessary measles pandemic is a new level of maddening](https://open.substack.com/pub/discourseblog/p/what-the-fuck-are-we-doing?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios)."
David Edmonston was 11 years old when, in 1954, doctors in Boston [collected samples](https://substack.com/redirect/ed028845-0cdf-4d34-ae85-4e975c285d43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from his throat and his blood. Their work eventually enabled the development of the world's first measles vaccine, and helped win the [Nobel Prize](https://substack.com/redirect/ce41a124-074b-4c2b-b90e-52b6049ca76f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Physiology or Medicine for Harvard professor John Franklin Enders.
Before the development of the measles vaccine [in 1963](https://substack.com/redirect/5bdfe12a-1454-4666-9390-1801302c1433?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the disease was both highly contagious and endemic across the United States. Each year, approximately 4 million Americans fell ill, and nearly 50,000 required hospitalization. Measles deaths in the U.S. [declined](https://substack.com/redirect/ed028845-0cdf-4d34-ae85-4e975c285d43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from 500 per year to none.
Today, Edmonston--whose blood helped create the measles vaccine--is 82 years old. Even though his attitudes towards vaccines have shifted over time and grew complicated during the COVID outbreak, he [said recently](https://substack.com/redirect/ed028845-0cdf-4d34-ae85-4e975c285d43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "vaccines are very important not just for the individuals who take them, but for our society as a whole."
Referencing federal regulators, Edmonston noted that "There's an awful lot of misinformation out there, and you need to be careful who you listen to."
As absurd as deaths from measles in 2025 may appear, they remain a signal of more to come.
According to Lauren Gardner, leader of Johns Hopkins University's databases tracking disease outbreaks, "What we're seeing with measles is a little bit of a ['canary in a coal mine](https://substack.com/redirect/53de6097-8df6-4d50-9e7c-1e02246837df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's indicative of a problem that we know exists with vaccination attitudes in this county and...[is] likely to get worse."
_Less than 1% of subscribers to this newsletter actively support its expansion. Please join us to enable me to write more frequently!_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..5MQfQRVtntj7jSoT4tHHaTubFGP2uj1SicXqhAc-YPU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168094097)
### **Prioritizing politics rather than survival**
Why have Texans been reduced to cannon fodder for the dumbest domestic decisions of the empire?
Even though the state is reeling from multiple preventable disasters, its elected leaders appear more focused on their own interests than those of their constituents. Within days of the floods, Texas Governor Greg Abbott published his [list of stated priorities](https://substack.com/redirect/475806b3-1c08-417b-9e9f-25377005f29c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the coming year.
While several items on the list related to building better early warning systems for "flood prone areas throughout Texas," his list also included a focus on [contriving a larger GOP majority in the U.S. House](https://substack.com/redirect/f0f02b17-3837-4bd2-af6e-2a253092210d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Representatives by gerrymandering the state even more than it already has been.
Last year, the Brennan Center for Justice at NYU [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/4dba484f-2cf1-4892-9947-80ef0ebc2736?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "When Texas Republicans redrew their state's congressional map [in 2024], they took what had been one of the most competitive and politically interesting maps in the country and, through discrimination against fast-growing racial and ethnic minorities, transformed it into one of the most boring and least dynamic." The Department of Justice sued the State of Texas the same week that report was released, alleging that the state legislature's gerrymander is intentionally racially discriminatory and violates the Voting Rights Act.
While his constituents are dying by the dozens from floods, and flooding hospitals due to a disease outbreak, Abbott's greatest concerns include building his party's majority by further degrading democracy within his state.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=168094097&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0MDk3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIyNTc1NjYsImV4cCI6MTc1NDg0OTU2NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KqRuKbqw3kT7DK0VV4UiSnRvEup_i3K7iX82WILraXA)
### **America 's future**
The unfolding debacles across Texas should expose the true nature of America's future intended by the Trump administration. We'll have plenty of ICE agents but not enough scientists, plenty of police but not enough medicine, plenty of weapons but not enough food.
Welcome to the future of America, in all its ugly and sordid glory
This is an era of institutional ignorance, with all the surprises that we could expect from erecting leaders who bury their heads in the sand.
It is difficult to convey the idiocy of the past week's events. There is not a single reason to dismiss the floods as an unpreventable calamity when the professionals who used to issue warnings made clear when they were fired that the system they staffed would suffer from inadequate staffing.
Nor does explaining reality constitute politicizing a disaster. It is simply a matter of connecting cause and effect--just like scientists (for whom the administration and its right wing enablers harbor such disdain) do every day.
_Paid subscribers can access evidence of a major social media platform algorithmically suppressing the availability of content by dissenting voices. This suppression tilts the discursive playing field, violates the vision of user-generated content[promoted](https://substack.com/redirect/a118b20c-d443-41a1-9924-dbbdeff45ccb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by social media companies in the early 2010s, and indicates why readers who share my articles play such a crucial part in the process of informing the public..._

name: Mikie Sherrill
subject: Proud to be Democratic nominee for NJ governor
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Hey friend, it’s Mark Kelly. My friend Mikie Sherrill is running for Governor in New Jersey and needs our help. I hope you’ll read the message below from her, and [**consider splitting a donation between Mikie's campaign and mine.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-sherrill.Ja8SSv&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=1&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)
---
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-sherrill.Ja8SSv&refcode=em250711-1-90&refcode2=6119.6688874.yPWhrb&t=2&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)
---
Team, I am so honored to be the official Democratic nominee for governor in New Jersey. Thank you to everyone who played a part in this victory. I wouldn’t be here without this incredible grassroots team who chipped in, volunteered, and voted.
And we can’t stop now.
We know what we are up against. The general election is underway — and if recent history is any indication, it’s going to be an expensive, hard-fought race decided by a razor-thin margin.
[**Will you help us get off to a strong start against Trump’s handpicked Republican nominee by splitting $3 between my campaign and Mark Kelly today?**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-sherrill.Ja8SSv&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=3&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)
**[DONATE](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-sherrill.Ja8SSv&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=4&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)**
Friend, I was raised to believe that we have an obligation to our community and our country.
It’s why I took my first oath to the Constitution as a teenager when I entered the Naval Academy.
It’s why I spent nearly a decade in the Navy as a helicopter pilot and Russian policy officer.
It’s why I went to law school, and became an outreach and reentry coordinator and a federal prosecutor.
It’s why I first ran for office — flipping a Republican-held congressional seat here in New Jersey that no one thought we could win at the time.
And it’s why I’m running to serve as our next governor. It’s too hard to get ahead in New Jersey, and too often, our kids have fewer opportunities than we did. The system feels broken, and Donald Trump’s agenda is only making it worse. I can’t accept that — and as governor, I won’t.
**But the fact is, New Jersey is now a battleground state, and Trump is _already_ campaigning for my opponent, Jack Ciattarelli, a career politician who has promised to pass an abortion ban, defund public schools, and bring DOGE to New Jersey. So, truthfully, we won’t win this race unless we have a strong grassroots team behind us right from the start. **
[**Friend, if you’re with me in this fight, I would be incredibly grateful if you could become a Founding Donor to our general election campaign by splitting a donation between my campaign and Mark Kelly today →**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-sherrill.Ja8SSv&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=5&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll instantly process your split contribution to Mikie Sherrill and Mark Kelly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1396?c0=mek-em-sherrill.GgGmHU&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=24&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1397?c0=mek-em-sherrill.XjvE6D&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=25&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1390?c0=mek-em-sherrill.fRukJ2&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=26&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1391?c0=mek-em-sherrill.MZrAAA&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=27&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1392?c0=mek-em-sherrill.B3Kv6M&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=28&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1395?c0=mek-em-sherrill.tfr74Y&refcode=em250711-1-90&t=29&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb)**
---
****
Thank you so much for your support,
Mikie

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=30&akid=6119%2E6688874%2EyPWhrb).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

**Peter, the housing crisis is _personal_ to me.**
Listen, I was priced out of my home while running for Congress, and was houseless for two months. When I made it to Congress and moved to D.C, I was denied from multiple apartment buildings because unfair housing policies tanked my credit. There was no way to win.

And it isn’t just me – this crisis is impacting my entire generation. Americans used to literally buy homes at my age. _Now, we can’t even afford to rent an apartment._

**Our housing system is clearly broken. But we don’t have to accept broken systems, or wait for Republicans who clearly don’t give a damn to change their tune. Housing IS a human right, and I won’t stop fighting until our basic needs are met.**

Trouble is, the same special interests behind this crisis are desperate to keep people like me out of Congress, where we can actually fight back with policy. **I need your help to hold this seat and hold them back, so I have to ask:**

[**_Will you donate $27 right now to help hit my weekly fundraising goal? Every dollar has the power to keep me fighting for all of us in Congress, and I know we can make actual change together. clean_body: | Team, TODAY is Kristen’s Birthday – and we want to make sure she knows how much we appreciate the work she’s been putting in for the people of MI-08. Between fighting back against the GOP’s cruel big ugly bill, withstanding attacks from the far-right, and pushing through a major FEC deadline – it’s been a rough and tumble few weeks for Kristen. She’s shown grit, heart, and determination every step of the way. It would mean the world to her to receive some well wishes from supporters like you, so add your name now: | Thanks for signing, Team KMR | We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to .
