name: Indiana GOP
subject: Empowering Main Street, Supporting Students, and Unleashing Energy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| | _**Empowering Main Street, Supporting Students, and Unleashing Energy**_
---
Friends, This has been yet another week of results for Hoosiers, with Governor Braun proving yet again that he can deliver on his promises. The administration began the week by launching the Indiana Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation to help Main Street entrepreneurs start and grow their businesses. Led by entrepreneur Brian Schutt, the office will focus on small business support, innovation, and expanding resources across Indiana, including rural and youth-focused efforts. This is a vital step towards spreading the benefits of economic development to all four corners of the state, with wage and job growth being the administration's central focus. On Tuesday, Governor Braun celebrated major education reforms with a signing ceremony highlighting universal school choice, increased teacher pay, more funding for classrooms, and a new A–F accountability system to measure student-focused outcomes. On Wednesday, Governor Braun released a report detailing the state’s work to eliminate divisive and discriminatory Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiative and replace them with programs focused on Merit, Excellence, and Innovation (MEI). Following a 2025 executive order, state agencies have reviewed and eliminated over 70 DEI programs and 200 related policies, redirecting focus toward fairness and performance. Governor Braun also celebrated key legislation to boost Indiana’s energy production and reliability, including a major push for nuclear power. His all-of-the-above energy strategy emphasizes producing affordable, reliable power to meet growing demand. As always, thank you for being a part of our team. Please feel free to reach out if there's anything I can assist with.
---
| |
---
| | Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman
---
|
---
| | |
---
---
| | _**Governor Braun Launches Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation**_
---
---
Governor Mike Braun has created the Indiana Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation to assist Main Street entrepreneurs in expanding their businesses and boosting Indiana’s economy. “Maureen and I lived the American dream of growing Main Street businesses in our hometown, and I want every Hoosier to have that opportunity.” \- Governor Mike Braun via a [press release](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/wsj5kMfbnjGd7Wxfcx3oAmPi-lehCKJGnjLJl34QqSpIjltfvLltNUsMkiLWs_rX1ltM2g7bHPJuPVPu02J0jU5vFA-dRZqBeDUAIJjs3csqbsk8OTqrlHqtB6gj8QNloTOtCrjEL2cTcBXQsYp22hkhnksnXXKLY39zA_-k-pDxacHqlW0wngJ_alLaTyxF_dVRskL3YL5IYCqY2R1Fki1wlr5m0oDp6MQKnnfH4xvE7iVY4kjzn5AHi5dQmuJbEdZdbQU0qrfLd9GipywZ6dh9rejhVWGhuZuV5lRWbmYepemym6Qg9ezmdeFGKCJq6eL_PKtS5t1CIyy6-Yie37QTVz87oXmI-vtv7JSRwNEwJ6cyLGUsAeplddKkF6Cmj56vx9KtiRAH9S9MYXE2q7L2XS_R2PabXE1OoDm41yF3H6oc51vI0DqM1lVWzUrcHKQgyJ27kXLu87ZK4mCjP-d4y4VkyRIu4Suko9SuHpZq9qCMxg8gKn86YAaPDHtrO6MtEBienGF0jJm_5dKclULBA2hVyMvHKTh50XKDDTJI5TjLKUTQG2Feg9TxNehJIs9ObO-4T8MXpi0pz2AHf5E0rZNoq9ESu2yZz20MxlkksUipzICdcYC4uYDYTS5gVxq8di8IVyaqF_7xSvIZnu8hNA/g9B2Mq0PtEWAP506LJhLpFXs620AzlIW/10)
---
_**Governor Braun Celebrates Education Wins**_
---
---
Governor Mike Braun held a signing ceremony to highlight major education wins, including universal school choice, higher teacher pay, more classroom funding, and A-F school accountability. The state budget boosts K-12 support by $640 million over two years. “Today we’re celebrating universal school choice, accountability, and new investment in our classrooms.” \- Governor Mike Braun via a [press release](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/hoK-DqQEuTtVnLcG-GiuPm6-GW-aqqMli9R8XuFepG0pPQZWMbllCRgQRDZIUjZHO9LKf8xoCa7YiAnGxnuSdR2RXAla348HeAIKy2AVHY1tqrvb2UGsxtlI-O1zUP0hFOVHjd4JdhAavsZ1aQFCcdXjnmqPmov-64WW54U3vOoSBR0Fwon37_vyTNc8E7sjjAjPFe2pUzaWohNKgK722fnCbGZkit75CqFiHfEGfUiw7KPR_eCMjRPsLMM-fpIQyUkqfpV1XPWyczzEuoJicZqtLRCX-k-PSoj9vEi6LcQqEhuLi5qnJxKWaPoNk45hcpLxu4n9hBfvxvxUQBmdooD3uB4P-etz6cniWFLMu4oNDqjndttuxkqqnutgP_PgIgimZgZJc7W2QkMEnYGni5Ne8Zk16G0bVjOaSLIAn6JYggFsvfMVbVv211OOzDvkIlCx85YL4wyidtK-NIOy4ZS4Su4dlevaWBKx05aiy0Db6k5wM75Jj-KIzhxlAqpfeyQujJIeMXXEi9h4rds9lOLpQRTI9cZB-SSV2UsfUfnCmZv-JrEXD2lg-aGoycALYWHArz_lRcY26HLWqydY/3MuAFgJnRC7rTtZcBfHwPzFeUAfA_40f/10)

_**Governor Braun Ends DEI Initiatives**_
---
---
Governor Mike Braun released a report detailing his administration’s progress in replacing DEI programs with a new focus on MEI, following his January 2025 executive order. "“Indiana has replaced the divisive, politically-charged DEI ideology with Merit, Excellence, and Innovation: a level playing field where every single Hoosier has the chance to get ahead with hard work." \- Governor Mike Braun via a [press release](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/6MGhdRVMHlasqapaKAKQBx_m0W-viUcyZu_z-6JWnIJtaxCHlFWMTs3wH3oJeMWeIhwZbEFBe5xeYuNjQb4FUC_4uIkT84A4jmuBbK2l7P9TeP5oZ_paMlgLTG7_7Y4lIopEeiZdSG6I7nO46k5zvVZfuNENSknsF0w6SJC1DL9TlimNfNZSYUaCkebp3dtm7V4cBj9xL371Xhc1LSIo69vDWTxEzY20sHFIgtS_nKhDM817wisAknBAG0jSNKdzTKL1NuVOFZtAggUb1qFKsEfO4bmYuXchkbTHKq6VxbSL0DKLEw5_WoWbegN4STlvm6Nv9KIwL63gPHIxIhm9askFU6rLLpiUQC_Gd4cn0aqtoWWhyKlQe1y6Tv1AHFPQUJKrZ8A4luwMKxwP0umHTU-Per-M5hSdXCKgcAJGHDmIGsGafxKczfFZJoa-sGxd_C13j_dNMuyVfT_TbMX1sHb6jDLnvQBOaLWL6-tBsCrS9cPnfyp1tD9xDLf4jPyfLSllRF2wYoejHrTg1u0rN6zVdLm2rmZ1Hc3ZPQPUzYjyeS78_mLv7l8WB9szpsRYw78xqtYzwM-07vI-xpsjW7nhf2kOoQ5eVT9QTIK88X5goo68fGr3pWe9r8JQWAFcqEMLwsBTFH8wR4JDvnzO6qnsbgeKP0oS1A5JIM-RsL0V7G511s2b3CsXq5pK0iLJtVcZ6QQbZFAxhOmGjps4LrKh8Bri/nSn-cgGg9gGyOSGD3U06w4r9fkZOpWMR/10)
---
_**Governor Braun Ramps Up Energy Production**_
---
---
Governor Braun’s energy plan focuses on reliable, affordable power through an all-of-the-above approach, with consumer protection at its core. Indiana is prepared to expand energy production, with laws aimed at cutting red tape and expanding state-of-the-art nuclear power. “As demand for electricity accelerates, the country will soon be divided into states that can meet this demand and those that can’t. We are stepping up to the challenge, expanding our energy portfolio, deploying nuclear energy, and innovating to power Indiana’s economy into the future, with affordability and reliability always at top of mind.” \- Governor Mike Braun via a [press release](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/HK4JoHvlwP82Lte6-JKxQr4uCKsHn0Wtt7eRyGfvLP948dzHQPqHe66djWaH3e18OL0UOpzxP_-bqDtKy0SuyDhF0pRHBiiEnRwlM7kYwAbOtV_Z9ywGDmeGFMTuDMc85I5N5rJTrPVnOs2oLTz1zbxUy_A0nzzfl0OnVyqwD2VmiMiVW8U1dDYiN6GO_UTAsh-gfxReS9-q3N__dF9292u9vsjbeWwu1ekyG07uqA9izbI1ZqksVFow5V7lNxM-EW1Xi7qDMRKgVgxPDaltejZGtO0JIlgsAgtNMZrq3r-cVi9lsQ-FTgmMxke4XwUtJ0tS_qtO8D2CQDwQMqG1wSDHvVqHJDii3ZrhnZlv0BM094dLhLmc73NZZYRtJm5cqXGJYKFLlrvBLIhcBsOTde3Q_pf7EATZHZy16fBlWWL3FjMoHwWVWd27mKU0VFbR1leW0V_g00mvhThq4a7lLxMGaacrOMhZrzGhx2xQdOb44GAYSjnfmcRfdiZ6fiTvsce287LV16QzHAQLV9DIOpbh46HogLZ73ja57YtFZYrpvusgID1pFwHhukKT5mEYIxxpEHpiYUzZITwPNdCgHGNfgt2GPg6YH8T2OxCzYyG5MmCXz41uC8D50n6Xm1DH_kdkjp-qZzr-MPeXHT7OEnHppRgn-U7iMIVaSci57NEVewDP93GU8IjCwDAWz7gmSIXUfKWSbDaLw-mATfX4GoLIhDjP/HQQ_WFBw6v24d60ShjNrga3bncsxX2zV/10)[](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/3__Y7aKUcji0PJiFvAHcGDBhwBuMSyrH_USi9DX4FVyMZqjmCEGcQdp3hwcItdk2rUElZVWFrEBX1dbUqqmo44BvGpDFSsj4Ib7M2V9ug8G03Zm42Qyh3-F_yAIxVW000ryWJkLMp5sjVMTALSvmQEUaW65l3-bzLFAvQ_FXQlAEyDVAvfmSAmsB0dY7Xyyr7xvrnVinhDFSXck-4Z5TeUpxB2bIirNTTq0yg8rqrt0lhwGwCRfl9d0ktkv_I_gcPDWgNJt0XSByDade-rDN4oRvB-RBBbi2TZhx7fIJ-jZ7A1My2styuGSzoC8iofSruW3T_hoRKTuF9_9iD5E7_Y15L4xA4qVVmFYwVs-E43yLtOfxSWbgNuFVq6ag9uPlP9aAaRkpzXjHVIp_xmHHGcWGLpUBlLCuW_A-HZkmZ4cp03cS2-PcwZWJyOZAW3Wio6gYjePdW2YDsr_zP4hVQ7ZZz7huoPsk6pQSbe-kyQ4WR03uENSfQtvTCExa2ypLKnuW8y71llFm0OMP06xoWmlRXUItI1QsCYG-tM5PQKlb5AHTVeJbKdIF0KntDLSD6SrFNXSImc5AVe_K_tkgmzs4DXl9woZmEagqMSbLit-Ir8s9kkGJ430gPI_lVxPx-FHrztFksaWf9pMYqkzAUo_jqW5SHP7VfHj4G7kJrKbA7uKZJas9NtHrn75N8xabZXvZMt1C9ArurW45-zPufi4DB99H/KMXcrf8Uv933SDzReEKXTjYbfKl6bPdD/10)

clean_body: | | | | | | | Empowering Main Street, Supporting Students, and Unleashing Energy Friends, This has been yet another week of results for Hoosiers, with Governor Braun proving yet again that he can deliver on his promises. The administration began the week by launching the Indiana Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation to help Main Street entrepreneurs start and grow their businesses. Led by entrepreneur Brian Schutt, the office will focus on small business support, innovation, and expanding resources across Indiana, including rural and youth-focused efforts. This is a vital step towards spreading the benefits of economic development to all four corners of the state, with wage and job growth being the administration's central focus. On Tuesday, Governor Braun celebrated major education reforms with a signing ceremony highlighting universal school choice, increased teacher pay, more funding for classrooms, and a new A–F accountability system to measure student-focused outcomes. On Wednesday, Governor Braun released a report detailing the state’s work to eliminate divisive and discriminatory Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiative and replace them with programs focused on Merit, Excellence, and Innovation (MEI). Following a 2025 executive order, state agencies have reviewed and eliminated over 70 DEI programs and 200 related policies, redirecting focus toward fairness and performance. Governor Braun also celebrated key legislation to boost Indiana’s energy production and reliability, including a major push for nuclear power. His all-of-the-above energy strategy emphasizes producing affordable, reliable power to meet growing demand. As always, thank you for being a part of our team. Please feel free to reach out if there's anything I can assist with. | | | | Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman | | | | | | Governor Braun Launches Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation Governor Mike Braun has created the Indiana Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation to assist Main Street entrepreneurs in expanding their businesses and boosting Indiana’s economy. “Maureen and I lived the American dream of growing Main Street businesses in our hometown, and I want every Hoosier to have that opportunity.” \- Governor Mike Braun via a Governor Braun Celebrates Education Wins Governor Mike Braun held a signing ceremony to highlight major education wins, including universal school choice, higher teacher pay, more classroom funding, and A-F school accountability. The state budget boosts K-12 support by $640 million over two years. “Today we’re celebrating universal school choice, accountability, and new investment in our classrooms.” \- Governor Mike Braun via a Governor Braun Ends DEI Initiatives Governor Mike Braun released a report detailing his administration’s progress in replacing DEI programs with a new focus on MEI, following his January 2025 executive order. "“Indiana has replaced the divisive, politically-charged DEI ideology with Merit, Excellence, and Innovation: a level playing field where every single Hoosier has the chance to get ahead with hard work." \- Governor Mike Braun via a Governor Braun Ramps Up Energy Production Governor Braun’s energy plan focuses on reliable, affordable power through an all-of-the-above approach, with consumer protection at its core. Indiana is prepared to expand energy production, with laws aimed at cutting red tape and expanding state-of-the-art nuclear power. “As demand for electricity accelerates, the country will soon be divided into states that can meet this demand and those that can’t. name: Navigating Uncertainty (by Vikram Mansharamani)
subject: Copper Cross Currents, Chile, and China
body:
Why America might need to pick from competing objectives

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..DdL5O8yFlHcxO8RFvqRizUs9Mufvdp9EzN_m-mC7i8Y?) for more

# [Copper Cross Currents, Chile, and China](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=168099064&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk5MDY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIyNjQxOTQsImV4cCI6MTc1NDg1NjE5NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.g1p7N8RJ-xI2yizxVwCuF4tEo8dipaLMZs3lq1KhFnI)
### Why America might need to pick from competing objectives
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Jul 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=168099064&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk5MDY0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjI2NDE5NCwiZXhwIjoxNzU0ODU2MTk0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.fmOYLh-pLfjLF2eZIkVIq8k8YhoYTJhiHLrPWs_sOtc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=168099064&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk5MDY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIyNjQxOTQsImV4cCI6MTc1NDg1NjE5NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.g1p7N8RJ-xI2yizxVwCuF4tEo8dipaLMZs3lq1KhFnI&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=168099064&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk5MDY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIyNjQxOTQsImV4cCI6MTc1NDg1NjE5NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.g1p7N8RJ-xI2yizxVwCuF4tEo8dipaLMZs3lq1KhFnI)
---
| | [](https://substack..QhCwHbwQJxmvLiuE60HdJK8b-XsjnTOnffmKAWRQaOI?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/copper-cross-currents-chile-and-china?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e47a3663-348d-4432-87ae-b71e29e5968f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Markets were again rattled this week when the on-again, off-again trade war appeared to be once again on. U.S. President Donald Trump [slapped](https://substack.com/redirect/cf43aca9-6dd6-4e20-b5d8-dc3aad2ce46b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a number of countries with tariffs, [announced](https://substack.com/redirect/c7057f05-aa74-4bc6-8a89-70d1252dbc76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the August 1st start date was firm, and [threatened](https://substack.com/redirect/59c00047-1ad6-4684-b45f-c151b1b468be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) tariffs on pharmaceuticals and copper as well.
The latter tariff target, copper, is of particular interest to me, because of its role in modern technology and therefore national security. Chile, which accounts for 23% of the world's copper supply, faces a major conundrum, one the United States has imposed on the South American nation. The country is the world's top producer of the metal for both the U.S. and China. Earlier this year, at a large mining conference in Santiago, I suggested that Chile would have to pick a side. Many Chilean CEOs and government leaders were very uncomfortable with this possibility, suggesting that they would instead seek to remain "neutral" and sell copper to everyone.
At one point during my visit, a mining company CEO was blunt: "Copper is our most important export. What would you do if you were in our shoes?"
My answer, which seemed to assuage his immediate concerns, was simple: "Sell copper, just don't sell the copper mines."
That was in April. While the advice remains "useful," it's not particularly helpful to the United States. Why's that? If the US market becomes more expensive to access, the extra Chilean copper will most likely be offered to China at lower prices.
* * *
## [Chile, China, & Copper: No Free Lunch](https://substack.com/redirect/1569fa4b-18ce-4e3b-b5a3-9d8fa7344365?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Apr 20
[](https://substack.com/redirect/1569fa4b-18ce-4e3b-b5a3-9d8fa7344365?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[This week I did a press interview with a reporter who writes for a Chilean newspaper. As we chatted, I was struck by the extent to which Chile may very well be Ground Zero of the current war between ...](https://substack.com/redirect/1569fa4b-18ce-4e3b-b5a3-9d8fa7344365?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/1569fa4b-18ce-4e3b-b5a3-9d8fa7344365?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
The Trump administration has been committed to an America First strategy and appears determined to reduce America's reliance of foreign supply chains for anything crucial to our economy or national security. Copper, a crucial component for electronics, certainly fits the bill on both accounts.
To understand why copper is so important, let's recall that all electronics and data centers, (and [therefore the artificial intelligence industry](https://substack.com/redirect/3feb0fe4-07e1-4ec5-9193-56a8b32cf7fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) use copper. Copper is literally a foundational ingredient for electrification and our technological future. And given US production (mainly in Arizona) is about one-fifth as large as Chile's and about two-thirds of China's output, copper is increasingly a strategic resource. Might this have been a reason for the Trump Administration's [desire](https://substack.com/redirect/b9ad7879-8ecb-403a-98d3-437e02c9d050?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to begin deep-sea mining?
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..Y0j5ZXVtZ9sEKSaiEHdGcEUK-jzhebUEHdG33fq-cCw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168099064)
Tariffs have the potential to achieve the America First agenda of self-reliance for the United States, but in the domain of copper production, that won't happen anytime soon. Instead, China will likely benefit from lower prices while the United States grapples with higher prices. In the long run, this may incentivize more US copper production, but let's not fool ourselves about what we mean by "long run." Even if we stumbled upon a new, undiscovered copper deposit within the United States (not something that seems particularly likely, but I'll concede it's a non-zero probability), [the average time it take to go from discovery to producing mine is more than 15 years](https://substack.com/redirect/5ee2504f-62e6-4d00-968e-490577ebb1b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
In the short run, then, America will be at competitive disadvantage in technological industries because of the copper tariffs.
* * *
## [America's Evolving Global Role](https://substack.com/redirect/506f4a74-05f2-42d3-a33e-eda17e656250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Feb 27
[](https://substack.com/redirect/506f4a74-05f2-42d3-a33e-eda17e656250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Since the day he descended an escalator to upend American politics, President Donald Trump has consistently delighted his supporters and confounded his critics. Seemingly on separate planets, these t...](https://substack.com/redirect/506f4a74-05f2-42d3-a33e-eda17e656250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/506f4a74-05f2-42d3-a33e-eda17e656250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
There's also another complication. I've written and spoken extensively about the "[Trump Doctrine](https://substack.com/redirect/f304b3bc-d2aa-4152-aa00-1ce491a9cc9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)", a modern-day version of the Monroe Doctrine, in which the United States prioritizes the Western Hemisphere and seeks to keep it free of adversary influences. The Trump Administration has appeared determined to push rivals out of the Western Hemisphere.
Unfortunately, copper tariffs seem likely to push Latin America (further?) into the arms of the Chinese. Chile will have no option other than to sell its copper to the Chinese, even at lower prices. This has the potential to weaken US-Chile ties and deepen Beijing's influence on Santiago. If Chile grows more dependent on China, it stands to reason that Washington's influence will wane.
* * *
[](https://substack.com/redirect/b564c887-f353-4377-b743-46be94436b02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [Geopolitics Trumps Economics](https://substack.com/redirect/b564c887-f353-4377-b743-46be94436b02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| March 11, 2024
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/b564c887-f353-4377-b743-46be94436b02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
The two Trump strategic goals of America First and a 21st century Monroe Doctrine are therefore at risk of cancelling each other out. Tariffs will push Chile to China _**without**_ lessening our dependence on foreign copper due to the long development timeline, all the while hampering our competitiveness by making anything electronic more expensive.
In the end, when it comes to copper and Chile at least, if the US doesn't prioritize one of these goals over the other, it might end up with neither.

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=168099064&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk5MDY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIyNjQxOTQsImV4cCI6MTc1NDg1NjE5NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.g1p7N8RJ-xI2yizxVwCuF4tEo8dipaLMZs3lq1KhFnI)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ffdbea62-ba6e-480b-a5c0-a7cff9114968?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/2a9ada5c-74bc-464e-b50d-e27f5c240f30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/4286fdeb-4338-48b9-910d-8f1e86197e3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/6340850b-1577-4401-99b5-c89c35a5076a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

In the short run, then, America will be at competitive disadvantage in technological industries because of the copper tariffs. | | | | ---|---|--- | Feb 27 | There's also another complication. I've written and spoken extensively about the "", a modern-day version of the Monroe Doctrine, in which the United States prioritizes the Western Hemisphere and seeks to keep it free of adversary influences. The Trump Administration has appeared determined to push rivals out of the Western Hemisphere. Unfortunately, copper tariffs seem likely to push Latin America (further?) into the arms of the Chinese. Chile will have no option other than to sell its copper to the Chinese, even at lower prices. This has the potential to weaken US-Chile ties and deepen Beijing's influence on Santiago. If Chile grows more dependent on China, it stands to reason that Washington's influence will wane. | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | March 11, 2024 | The two Trump strategic goals of America First and a 21st century Monroe Doctrine are therefore at risk of cancelling each other out. Tariffs will push Chile to China without lessening our dependence on foreign copper due to the long development timeline, all the while hampering our competitiveness by making anything electronic more expensive. In the end, when it comes to copper and Chile at least, if the US doesn't prioritize one of these goals over the other, it might end up with neither. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and **. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. Invite your friends and earn rewards If you enjoy Navigating Uncertainty, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Kelan Publishing, LLC PO BOX 932, Lincoln, NH 03251 ? ?

We're planning to expand our Impeach Trump NOW billboard to Goldman's New York City district next week - with more billboards to be announced depending on fundraising. Rep. name: The Feel Good Flyover
subject: Hero Rookie Guardsman, Music for Animals, and a Purr-Engine
body:
On his very first rescue mission, Coast Guard Petty Officer Scott Ruskan, 26, [helped save nearly 200 people](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2uqzAQBOCngW6RWQg_hYvb0OW2p0TGXoOTBSPbhPD2R8krnHqk0Xzzcx_vyrHb5v9qJWlnOybrx43OyJQShXGh4IP3T0fzoYKJq9rWIzptRxR4A9FCWY5GOb7GOjfSEiFVOcmyvSE2ddVhvsjedEarvrJUd5VuplZjZftpqoXqul6I3MlPm2jLEoVosCywtxU1NdkOVa_1lNWCjEs-FA_vtrSQ5cu_KBTarznLJaU9ZtW_DIcMh_M8C-vfH8Unz3A4YobDRic8KES6QHsVE3xFEE-3rhQgUNQHRdhIBb4AhYCd_M4EhpThCxK9VQTLKi5g2XsT4emYyUDT5UGa_XTMLjZtVot5VY6_49KfX31J_A0AAP__aOWRKQ) from catastrophic flash floods in central Texas.

| | |
---
| [](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0cGKpDAYBOCniTeb-EejffBg03janl1Y2D6Gv80fzU5iJIkjPU-_9LBvMKc6FHxQ1P2mbmidXec39NSb2ahsglrpSI5ypqgWiiGG8G5p3jHq5HH1e7KTUcChKXlbVpXSaN1T1YXuDRGQKKiv2gZA1qKDYuk7wUVLIA3pqm4M6Qc0QkxiajUaEHVh-5fG26oCziVUJzgbQbIm0wGep-nBak7a5hBPf4Nd80LGPcMHxdMUfOH6JectMTEwGBmMx3Gc0ONnWF81g_Fif27ZevtJMZU3nFdKdvflJRK-5yWGfV7K3_u2OfK05vJi0-yek10xE4NRby-Cd_dfP-71nzcmRo-YmLi-QqGe1YWLFupGcD5ehqG6NsO5laPk12GoZNd0Cg1uCh-JgYxkFBNXRPWlgMw4_7eK2OvtsM7ZJFtW89mjdV8L87ev-ejhXwAAAP__GVCjjA) | |
# Friday, July 11, 2025
---|---
**Good Afternoon!** When deadly floodwaters swept through a Central Texas summer camp over the Fourth of July, [rookie Coast Guard swimmer Scott Ruskan](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2uqzAQBOCngW6RWQg_hYvb0OW2p0TGXoOTBSPbhPD2R8krnHqk0Xzzcx_vyrHb5v9qJWlnOybrx43OyJQShXGh4IP3T0fzoYKJq9rWIzptRxR4A9FCWY5GOb7GOjfSEiFVOcmyvSE2ddVhvsjedEarvrJUd5VuplZjZftpqoXqul6I3MlPm2jLEoVosCywtxU1NdkOVa_1lNWCjEs-FA_vtrSQ5cu_KBTarznLJaU9ZtW_DIcMh_M8C-vfH8Unz3A4YobDRic8KES6QHsVE3xFEE-3rhQgUNQHRdhIBb4AhYCd_M4EhpThCxK9VQTLKi5g2XsT4emYyUDT5UGa_XTMLjZtVot5VY6_49KfX31J_A0AAP__aOWRKQ) stepped up, calm, steady, and barely a day on the job. In the middle of panic and loss, Ruskan led rescues that saved over 165 people, offering quiet courage in the middle of the storm. Read more below. Love what you're reading? Don't miss the video podcast _Feel Good Flyover_ on [Spotify](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0M1OxCAUQOGnobtp4Jb-LVi4sAvj1Gg0RjeEoZcWpdAAM5P69GZ8Bfcn3-K8H-VRWWf9PKoVhZmNzCZIj9fkMGeMcsEYYgjfFuezilNalV_PyWojgUJ9oO2BMTkp63bJi0kYRMCqQMHaGqDhVQfFIoBzPfXQdYrWHJsT9nVnal3R_qRbg3VhxU2jLWNAaQOshN5U2HA0Hahe6xPhFCebQyy_gvV5QeP2cMFY6rAWTiw5b4lUdwQGAkPY0JdpC9ma_RYQGNISrgQGPl5eHp5-3PPo5rfx8fXD3_fss4hi2q7WOZualnA6r8q6Pzn_e8lFwG8AAAD__7Lrd0g), [YouTube](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFu8yAYgOHTmC0WfGCwB4Z_8ZZI_9QRYfiwabGJACdNT1-1V-gB3ld63q7mamOKx3qzO-qwBtNCNgc-a8LWsJgNSy45f0RcT1t83e2xnzW6YIDCcKHqwpjxNqaXEcTrgAjICWqmBgAp-Ahk09xZaZ3kYhgHxyzFCbzzAuikhB39QqL-uVHFGFAqgfUwBY5SYBjBTs4tnaDoY8ulf8_xaBuG9MoPLL3LO0l6a-1eO_6vg7mD-ZXPdvYLdjB_3cTnvPxXrK6kaH9_xpRilaoTdN1tTL99-zP8oeE7AAD__yZtbkA), or [Apple ](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAURL8Gd7bggu2koHiNu7x2S3QNF5tdbCzAifL3q6xWygdsOzM6I52Pm7lhiGFf_uNG2i_eVJ_MTo8SqVbKZqWcckpfgZYTsysb7ttZgvUGOPQtH1shjMMQn0Y1TnsiINmQFmMPMCh5gWbV2As5W2-tQo_USyV6OYtRzCO5WQ3YBP2i8VEI4HwA0cHVSxoU-Qvg1dqZKU4u1JS7zxT2upKPz3Sn3Nm0NVGvtR6FyX8MJgbTkZzFUkuHxxHpNWEwneXdMJjqSu0vo32nwYmL4CDhOogma3c8QoyhDCNTfNkwxJ-_-mdRdw3fAQAA__-tWn7x)(audio only), where host Amy Hess brings you even more behind-the-scenes stories, visuals, and conversations. We're sponsored today by [Magnesium Breakthrough by BiOptimizers](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0cGKpDAYBOCniTeb-EejffBg03janl1Y2D6Gv80fzU5iJIkjPU-_9LBvMKc6FHxQ1P2mbmidXec39NSb2ahsglrpSI5ypqgWiiGG8G5p3jHq5HH1e7KTUcChKXlbVpXSaN1T1YXuDRGQKKiv2gZA1qKDYuk7wUVLIA3pqm4M6Qc0QkxiajUaEHVh-5fG26oCziVUJzgbQbIm0wGep-nBak7a5hBPf4Nd80LGPcMHxdMUfOH6JectMTEwGBmMx3Gc0ONnWF81g_Fif27ZevtJMZU3nFdKdvflJRK-5yWGfV7K3_u2OfK05vJi0-yek10xE4NRby-Cd_dfP-71nzcmRo-YmLi-QqGe1YWLFupGcD5ehqG6NsO5laPk12GoZNd0Cg1uCh-JgYxkFBNXRPWlgMw4_7eK2OvtsM7ZJFtW89mjdV8L87ev-ejhXwAAAP__GVCjjA), offering a breakthrough Magnesium supplement with a special upcoming deal for Amazon Prime Day.

## Rookie Guardsman Saves 165 in Texas Flood
On his very first rescue mission, Coast Guard Petty Officer Scott Ruskan, 26, [helped save nearly 200 people](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2uqzAQBOCngW6RWQg_hYvb0OW2p0TGXoOTBSPbhPD2R8krnHqk0Xzzcx_vyrHb5v9qJWlnOybrx43OyJQShXGh4IP3T0fzoYKJq9rWIzptRxR4A9FCWY5GOb7GOjfSEiFVOcmyvSE2ddVhvsjedEarvrJUd5VuplZjZftpqoXqul6I3MlPm2jLEoVosCywtxU1NdkOVa_1lNWCjEs-FA_vtrSQ5cu_KBTarznLJaU9ZtW_DIcMh_M8C-vfH8Unz3A4YobDRic8KES6QHsVE3xFEE-3rhQgUNQHRdhIBb4AhYCd_M4EhpThCxK9VQTLKi5g2XsT4emYyUDT5UGa_XTMLjZtVot5VY6_49KfX31J_A0AAP__aOWRKQ) from catastrophic flash floods in central Texas. The [New Jersey native](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDHOozAQhU9jOkdmcExSuNiGLttuiRx7DLMxGNkD2dx-Ra7wN1PMkz697_15jA9Hidbpt1vQximOHPO44rsmZMYyzlhyyflFOO2uhLq4ddkr-TiCgqtUvWzbMThKn1E3wUZEwK5B2_ZXAKO7GzSzNSbqGJ4udh2A6o3x4daqqJ_Ba3D3a0P2pKm-bUEpA-0F7rFDozHewN29fwqtMBDncvmbaeUZY_rkA8vF56VJdmbequh-CRgEDOtny5XPSMBwggUMqj-PETDsVZ5-AgafXWX59ZJuwULerfIUltVnZln2-vp-0lZldQdKxn-uyphyDvIgz7ScnKbYsL0pJaqmF1pNi6P0bcY_nvSw8D8AAP__r1ONjw) and former accountant was part of an aerial rescue team that responded to Camp Mystic, a Christian girls' camp hit hard by the Guadalupe River's rise over the Fourth of July. With roads washed out and boats unable to reach the site, Ruskan stayed on the ground to [triage survivors](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uwyAUhZ8Gb1hwTfwzMHTxlq4dEcEXmwb7RoDj5u0rp4_Q7UhHOkff93U1Vxti2OZPu6L2szfFk9nwyBFLwWQWTJSI7gHn3aYpr3Zb9xycNyDgwkXHpTSTDfFlVDVpjwjYVKhldwFoVdNDtejW3izAcBE9ul512LtuGIQfQDrV9I2qgj7XRCclCNGCrGHwDbYKfQ92cO7GlMApFEr1N4WtLOjji56YakdrFfVSyiOz5oPByGA8jqN2t3xSnD2DccPjRen-lzKDMTsqhac93-3GHdlc-JuPF_yxmftINPGE2e3IYKySnh5HiDHktmNKzKsN8X1d_u3sqeE3AAD__xKMhAQ) and guide helicopter crews. He said his role quickly shifted from emergency response to comforting scared campers and grieving parents, many still in pajamas. Working alongside Army Blackhawks and Coast Guard MH-65s, Ruskan directed hours of high-risk evacuations. Despite national praise, including [a shout-out](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFywyAQheHToM4eWBBIBUUadXaTIiVD0K5EgoQHsB3fPqNcId2bV3zF_3FxFx9T3Jer39DSQq5Rdjs-a8LWsLgVSy45f0dc7r7MdfP7dq8xkAMO_YmbkxBu9jG9nOpmS4iAskMrTA-glRygW60K3gAnOfARZy8GTaonGQbvRU-iF120h8aNEMC5BnGGkSRqhTSAH0P4ZIrjHFsu568c97YipVd-YDmHvHXJrq3dKpNvDCYG08_xMpjeMbhrxmPW5tu9MpjEqIThkkttoB80KC67YufbM6YUqzZM8WXzMf3B7d9FHhZ-AwAA___Vi3PM) from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, he downplayed his role, saying, _" Any one of us, if anyone else was on duty that day, they would have done the same thing as us."_ __📣 Today's Feel Good Flyover Podcast shares exclusive rescue footage. ([Watch Now](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO4yAQxvGnwV0sGIidFBQnRe4S6aor0cTM2NxiEwGON_v0q-wrbPt90q_4_7u6K4YY1umGC1me2FVObqW9RKqVspspp5zSR6Bpw-zLguuylTCyAwnHg-wPSjmPIb6cabxlIiDdkFX9EaAz-gTNbPnYIzN6D0CKFcuONaM28ozGIPgm2Lcme6VAyg5UC2fW1BniE-B5HO_CSPKhptz-T2GtM3F8pSfldkxLE-1c66MI_UfAIGDY9719pa1ud3r_7wXrOAs9PIW-fN3M53D_26syCeiq0BelS5Otf-whxlC6Xhg5LRjiD15_XeVp4TsAAP__R-t32Q)) __
## Principal Leads With Kindness
Zac Bauermaster, a 39-year-old [elementary school principal](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGypCAQRb9GM6agZVADgk0mm003tBAa7V0UC1pn5-9fzfuFF9-6p06dP8_p6SjRvvx2G9q4xIljnnZ81YTMWKYVSy45_yNcTldC3dy-nZV8nEDCXcheKDUFR-k96TbYiAjYtWhVfwcwuhugXW0HMPtBxruJOGszdhC19tFFp4fOzLol-6HJXimQ0oC6wRg7NBrjAG70fm60xECcy-1vpp1XjOmdLyw3n7c22ZX5qE33q4FHA48D85HwMzXwuKi4JI5Cu6fDJYF7PQtW8XkLLaWofAbcuYrVXSi4nJUxCBfOxMKxqH7NOQn879NZ6UKhVG90L8e22HC8KCWqpm-0XDZH6duHfxzysvAVAAD__9MVi78) in Lancaster County, Pennsylvania, is gaining national attention for his upbeat hallway routines and deep commitment to student well-being. [His videos](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGu2yAUxvGnwZstOIBxBoa2qdWh6doRETjYtGAijGPl7a9yX-HuP3369P97MzcbU9yWPzajDkswLRSz4bknbA2rWbGWWsr_iMthq9-z3fKxRxcMUJA9VT1jxtuYXkZ0XgdEQN6hZkoCjIJP0K3aqTtOVkhrJZVSSYUI0k8WEcVkueuifq9RxRhQOgIb4BI4jgLDBPbi3J0Iij62Uod_JW5txZBe5Yl1cCV3Sa-tPXbCvxGYCczneQ5x25tdqs1vQWCuiInAfP1l-E_z_fU7_3hbPh8tm70c1SHh17gYzHf0BMa4mBo94VcHijMQoheOqV7QUfWTVL63ivPAmbgwzruq_eOMKcV9VETQJduYPq-1Lzd9avgIAAD__9NiiUo), showcasing high-fives, fist bumps, and warm morning greetings, have drawn millions of views, but his focus remains on building a school where kids and staff want to come each day. From Fist Bump Fridays to breakfast with students who show kindness, Bauermaster is creating a culture rooted in trust, not just test scores. He credits his own struggles with anxiety for shaping his approach, saying he makes it a point to show up on the hard days because that's when students need him most.
## Chihuahua Guides Glacier Rescue in Alps
A hiker who fell 26 feet into a crevasse on Switzerland's Fee Glacier was rescued last Friday [thanks to his Chihuahua](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGupSAYhJ8GO07wlwNaUGxjd7bd0qD86L-LcALoiW-_8Sb3CW4zzUy-zMyf1_SyFCiuv-2Oxq9-qj5NET8lYK2Ypw1zyin9I1wPm13ZbdyPQoufQMCTC83bdnKWwjXJxhmPCNg1aFr9BFCy66HZjJIOZtkiOr08tVSdmwc1OLUo6KHXuiFz04RuWxBCQfuAwXeoJPoe7LAsM5MCHdWUH38TxbqhD1c6MT-WtDfBbLW-C-t-MRgZjPF6p1Jvi8F4gxmMQn9LII-lXgEZjPc6vmy0HXY7LC_2xMLTJ2Iu_M5x6ytmbjko7lOqvB77HJBTrIlbnu1J8eY02bj3h0KgojSTYt0tha9u9cenngb-BwAA___5k40R), which led search crews to the nearly invisible hole. The man had plunged through a snow bridge and used a walkie-talkie to call for help, but it was his dog's steady presence atop a nearby rock that helped rescuers locate him, according to [Air Zermatt](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO8yAQhJ8Gd1hmTWynoPgbd_nbK9EaL4Y7bCLAQbmnPyXV9dfNSKPRzPdx0zf0wR_bf9xJ2c3qYqM-qOZApVDSjlJMMX552k5Ma97x2M_sjdXQwYV3IxdCr-jDU8tmVZYIqG9IifECMMh-gsYpiYuRi7CTwGUxYy_NOFnx8mZEMqLx6tXWjUJA1w0gWrjangZJdgK8GrMw2dHqS0ztZ_RHcWTDMz4otSbuTVCulHtm_T8GM4O51tqiT_yb0o6ltMYxmOlgML-OMZiN8-5EdyLPLtbMf4V5ccQrPnmJb2kSPTBn4rLvm6TWe_Uh-DyMTHbbjj68F5Q_o3so-AkAAP__nw-HDg), the Swiss helicopter rescue team. clean_body: On his very first rescue mission, Coast Guard Petty Officer Scott Ruskan, 26, from catastrophic flash floods in central Texas. | | | | | | Friday, July 11, 2025 ---|--- Good Afternoon! When deadly floodwaters swept through a Central Texas summer camp over the Fourth of July, stepped up, calm, steady, and barely a day on the job. In the middle of panic and loss, Ruskan led rescues that saved over 165 people, offering quiet courage in the middle of the storm. Read more below. Love what you're reading? Don't miss the video podcast Feel Good Flyover on , , or only), where host Amy Hess brings you even more behind-the-scenes stories, visuals, and conversations. We're sponsored today by , offering a breakthrough Magnesium supplement with a special upcoming deal for Amazon Prime Day. | Rookie Guardsman Saves 165 in Texas Flood On his very first rescue mission, Coast Guard Petty Officer Scott Ruskan, 26, from catastrophic flash floods in central Texas. The and former accountant was part of an aerial rescue team that responded to Camp Mystic, a Christian girls' camp hit hard by the Guadalupe River's rise over the Fourth of July. With roads washed out and boats unable to reach the site, Ruskan stayed on the ground to and guide helicopter crews. He said his role quickly shifted from emergency response to comforting scared campers and grieving parents, many still in pajamas. Working alongside Army Blackhawks and Coast Guard MH-65s, Ruskan directed hours of high-risk evacuations. Despite national praise, including from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, he downplayed his role, saying, " Any one of us, if anyone else was on duty that day, they would have done the same thing as us." 📣 Today's Feel Good Flyover Podcast shares exclusive rescue footage. () Principal Leads With Kindness Zac Bauermaster, a 39-year-old in Lancaster County, Pennsylvania, is gaining national attention for his upbeat hallway routines and deep commitment to student well-being. , showcasing high-fives, fist bumps, and warm morning greetings, have drawn millions of views, but his focus remains on building a school where kids and staff want to come each day. From Fist Bump Fridays to breakfast with students who show kindness, Bauermaster is creating a culture rooted in trust, not just test scores. He credits his own struggles with anxiety for shaping his approach, saying he makes it a point to show up on the hard days because that's when students need him most. Chihuahua Guides Glacier Rescue in Alps A hiker who fell 26 feet into a crevasse on Switzerland's Fee Glacier was rescued last Friday , which led search crews to the nearly invisible hole. The man had plunged through a snow bridge and used a walkie-talkie to call for help, but it was his dog's steady presence atop a nearby rock that helped rescuers locate him, according to , the Swiss helicopter rescue team. While most teams rely on working breeds, this proved just as critical by staying put and leading rescuers when technology failed to pinpoint the location. | Flying together with our sponsor It 's Feel Good Friday -- and if you've been waiting for a sign to finally upgrade your sleep, stress, and mood... this is it. Time and again, history has shown us that far-right politicians and their billionaire backers are committed to restricting voting freedom. The RNC was always going to relentlessly defend the Trump Regime’s roadblocks that make voting harder by rolling back vote-by-mail and imposing complicated proof-of-citizenship requirements.
Are we surprised? No. But, it’s still **alarming** to constantly confirm that some of the very people who claim to serve Americans admit that the only way they win elections is when the freedom to vote is limited.
[**When extremist Republicans abuse their power to restrict voting freedom, we don’t back down — we fight. Ahead of the 2025 and 2026 elections, attacks from the far-right to undermine democracy will only get worse, and we need your help. Make a contribution to Fair Fight today, and if you’re able, take it a step further and become a monthly recurring donor.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111328287/556862279/375288700?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250711_T1_RepubVoterAccess_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjY2ZThjNC05MzVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-YwQqYGK-O3Aj0QpwCec3IDwFpVgtvryrq3vxkNIkkQ=)

**Georgians are well aware of the anti-voter agenda fueled by the far-right.** When Stacey Abrams ran for Governor in 2018, her opponent and then Secretary of State Brian Kemp oversaw his own election. Leading up to the election, his office purged nearly 700,000 voters and put 53,000 voter registrations on hold (70% of those voters were Black). **His office even closed 200 polling places in Black, brown, and low-income communities. **
Then in 2021, when a Texas-based right-wing group called True The Vote (TTV) challenged the eligibility of more than 250,000 voters in Georgia's 2021 Senate runoff elections, we took them to court — and despite concluding that the group’s methods “utterly lacked reliability” and “verge[d] on recklessness,” the court ruled that TTV did not violate the Voting Rights Act.
But Fair Fight appealed that decision and remains committed to preventing TTV from carrying out a voter suppression playbook and serving as a model for election conspiracists nationwide. Back in May, a federal circuit court heard our appeal.
**Now, we are waiting for the court’s decision, which could have major consequences for Republican extremists prepared to push their anti-voter agenda even further.**
[**Fair Fight is fighting to help deliver on the promise of equal access to the ballot box. Make a contribution now >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111328290/556862282/375288700?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250711_T1_RepubVoterAccess_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZjY2ZThjNC05MzVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-YwQqYGK-O3Aj0QpwCec3IDwFpVgtvryrq3vxkNIkkQ=)

clean_body: Your grassroots support will help us fight back against Republicans trying to restrict voting freedom. | | | Hey friend, Every day, there is new evidence of the GOP’s anti-voter, anti-democracy agenda. Just look at this headline from last month: | = Time and again, history has shown us that far-right politicians and their billionaire backers are committed to restricting voting freedom. The RNC was always going to relentlessly defend the Trump Regime’s roadblocks that make voting harder by rolling back vote-by-mail and imposing complicated proof-of-citizenship requirements. Are we surprised? No. But, it’s still alarming to constantly confirm that some of the very people who claim to serve Americans admit that the only way they win elections is when the freedom to vote is limited. = = = Georgians are well aware of the anti-voter agenda fueled by the far-right. When Stacey Abrams ran for Governor in 2018, her opponent and then Secretary of State Brian Kemp oversaw his own election. Leading up to the election, his office purged nearly 700,000 voters and put 53,000 voter registrations on hold (70% of those voters were Black). His office even closed 200 polling places in Black, brown, and low-income communities. Then in 2021, when a Texas-based right-wing group called True The Vote (TTV) challenged the eligibility of more than 250,000 voters in Georgia's 2021 Senate runoff elections, we took them to court — and despite concluding that the group’s methods “utterly lacked reliability” and “verge[d] on recklessness,” the court ruled that TTV did not violate the Voting Rights Act. But Fair Fight appealed that decision and remains committed to preventing TTV from carrying out a voter suppression playbook and serving as a model for election conspiracists nationwide. Back in May, a federal circuit court heard our appeal. Now, we are waiting for the court’s decision, which could have major consequences for Republican extremists prepared to push their anti-voter agenda even further. = If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- In service, Fair Fight = | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2025 Fair Fight - All rights reserved. name: VoteVets
subject: Stop Artificial Intelligence at the VA
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
At worst, it’s rejecting valid claims from Veterans who served this country and came home with wounds, injuries, and conditions related to their service.
#
Peter — something genuinely terrifying is happening at the Department of Veterans Affairs.
DOGE took a chainsaw to the VA, and that’s no secret. But most people don’t know how deep the damage goes. Some of the worst of it has to do with their implementation of untested, unproven, and unsafe artificial intelligence programs.
AI is screening everything from contracts to claims for disability. At best, this AI screening is cancelling contracts that are vital to the operations of the VA. At worst, it’s rejecting valid claims from Veterans who served this country and came home with wounds, injuries, and conditions related to their service.
This is how Trump, Collins, and DOGE want the VA to work — nameless, faceless algorithms using the same techniques as exploitative private health insurance companies to deny care to people who need and deserve it. That’s how they’re closing the capacity gaps as they continue to reduce 30,000 staff by September.
It’s outrageous and it has to stop.
[**Please, join VoteVets and tell Secretary Collins: Get Artificial Intelligence out of the Department of Veterans Affairs — and give Veterans the care they earned!**](https://act.votevets.org/signup/250711_vv_em_va_ai/?source=em250711-1-120&akid=34474%2E3013755%2EpRzmpA)
| [GET AI OUT OF THE VA](https://act.votevets.org/signup/250711_vv_em_va_ai/?source=em250711-1-120&akid=34474%2E3013755%2EpRzmpA)
---

The team at VoteVets

name: Katie Porter
subject: Trump will cost Covered CA enrollees $101 per month
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=39888%2E3422442%2EYov7kM)
Peter,
Because of Trump’s cuts to health care, Californians who get their health insurance through Covered California will pay **an average of $101 more per month beginning next year.**
Let me break it down for you: Covered California is our state’s Affordable Care Act marketplace. Millions of Californians purchase health care through it. The federal government normally invests in the program, so that people are able to afford care.
But now, all enrollees will see an increase in their monthly premium payment, with an average of $101 more per person beginning in January 2026. What’s worse, the people with the lowest incomes will see the biggest increase in monthly premiums. **Californians who make less than $61,000 per year are projected to pay $191 more per month.**
And just to be cruel and pay for tax breaks for billionaires—Republicans also made changes to eliminate automatic renewals of your health insurance and are limiting special enrollment periods. This just makes it more difficult for busy, working people to get care.
**Covered California expects that these higher costs and added enrollment barriers will force 600,000 Californians to lose the coverage they currently have.** _And this doesn’t even include the cuts to Medicaid, which I’ll talk about in another email._
These healthcare cuts that were part of Republicans’ big bill mean higher costs for Californians. People will get sicker when they can’t afford care. Families will face debt as medical bills pile up.
**[As governor, I’ll do everything I can to make care more accessible and affordable for Californians. But right now, I’m asking you to sign my petition to tell Republicans we won’t stand for this:](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/em_ms_kp_20250711_1_ak?source=em20250711-39888&akid=39888%2E3422442%2EYov7kM)**
[Sign the Petition](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/em_ms_kp_20250711_1_ak?source=em20250711-39888&akid=39888%2E3422442%2EYov7kM)
Thanks for all you do,
Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20250711-39888&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=39888_3422442_Yov7kM&akid=39888%2E3422442%2EYov7kM)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=39888&t=4&akid=39888%2E3422442%2EYov7kM)

Katie Porter for Governor 2026

Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026

Friends, In case you missed it, ICE just had the audacity to put words in my mouth, and I’m not letting it slide. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111328518/556863137/2005830277?refcode=250711_Seth_MFC_ICE&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYmE3ODcyYS05NTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N48ZocR36jAJJ7sQVjMM4dsuzB_qG7tiW38qRf9Vn0U=) When I heard that an ICE processing facility in my own district was being used to detain people in unacceptable conditions for days on end, I went to see it for myself. And what I saw was completely inappropriate – inhumane for long-term detention. I went on the record with my concerns, yet instead of simply acknowledging the truth, ICE got defensive. Every day we're seeing and hearing of abuses by ICE. Now, with a historic budget, they'll be more embolded than ever. This administration needs aggressive oversight by Congress, but the Republican majority isn’t going to let that happen. **We need to win again, but I need your help.** My criticisms have made me a target. I need your support to keep fighting for accountability and transparency in Washington. [**Can you chip in $5—or whatever you can—to support this fight and help elevate my message?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111328519/556863138/2005830277?refcode=250711_Seth_MFC_ICE&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYmE3ODcyYS05NTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N48ZocR36jAJJ7sQVjMM4dsuzB_qG7tiW38qRf9Vn0U=) Thank you for standing with me today. We won’t let the Trump administration intimidate those who speak the truth. Thank you,
Seth [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111328520/556863139/1866411932?refcode=250711_Seth_MFC_ICE&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYmE3ODcyYS05NTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N48ZocR36jAJJ7sQVjMM4dsuzB_qG7tiW38qRf9Vn0U=)

name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: Have you answered your call to duty?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
I’m in this race, not because I want to impress anyone in particular, but because I believe in a life of service, and mine is just getting started. | [](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=9fd8f503417e468e9ad434010fbc26f5&_e=Q2QhtOs7iHx__8GaYdG3M1UzBTpdM1S96Y8dog1rn-IPgd0LsHSgtTQ3KOdX1S1DmnW0QWCQLw84c0OUXxazw0PcTjfS1PBQv8Smj1HHi3jY4g5aFh3tV6lY7uni1qIngzaDRuyl0l7bvAML6P1WGyagKGMD2sc57ZvwqorBx2C4XlR3lpBNCQ7dXLpwnfBxOPBQwMMK-gjnh22dqZF9xiAzGH_uoF_NA7UuMAUtJU4t8t37J6bhp6wnu4g1ekrBIlfyu_NZf2p3tps0yeDvpwc-w6Tzd_1HeuQT_N2IbxGpvXveHOVvTvXyO888n_s6v_vhd7zV49yJDCWreNGlQYJljv8y_Zq-XHyclSTGIfpAZ9zGpeAdPK6Sw8VD0vMJOn6Rqye7IjZhym3iZjF9k4w3RGGVTq1Z2SiyQS1y-1WH99Andk7y7S62W4IJS2O5UhHGgQ1z4YLMQmsWyxBXmo2M8BlgER2uNrViEJ-WCBNJTBkc09bnmyjGNFeUUeQCLSLegxvCjpDVlw-HdyV_nTxI7QG9jIOg7JIEzmsv3944g7srNOCzLnTPhwml_mXXI7kf80AX_DWr6RpTHGgIqJrJ04o29uDJYzoTqG0SmzDtuQZfC8iALTOBp5UsCY5Xrd_c2Qcv-8ybD61GRK1vacf_0O7UKOuhDkApkMB-Y6-TSGFijzIsjLeaEUAMoIgUWy-JJiLYfMoYz4Nls7MIliYwChDUWVzA5UtZRay3U-s84qdyHhDWuSu0AnoPSvfmlruOVd8lz8akbu-75wusRZoAMNKJh2D6LI6vMjAgRYnlZ3DA_CHPseu1AhW7hlw-)

Fellow Conservative,

I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to.

Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: _**Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss.**_ While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos.

Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away.

I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— _**just weeks after 9/11.**_

_**I didn’t wait. I went.**_

And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map.

I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter.

Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign.
_**

[**HELP ME PROTECT THE AMERICA WE LOVE
CHIP IN $10 TODAY**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=9fd8f503417e468e9ad434010fbc26f5&_e=Q2QhtOs7iHx__8GaYdG3M1UzBTpdM1S96Y8dog1rn-IPgd0LsHSgtTQ3KOdX1S1DmnW0QWCQLw84c0OUXxazw0PcTjfS1PBQv8Smj1HHi3jY4g5aFh3tV6lY7uni1qIngzaDRuyl0l7bvAML6P1WGyagKGMD2sc57ZvwqorBx2C4XlR3lpBNCQ7dXLpwnfBxOPBQwMMK-gjnh22dqZF9xiAzGH_uoF_NA7UuMAUtJU4t8t37J6bhp6wnu4g1ekrBIlfyu_NZf2p3tps0yeDvpwc-w6Tzd_1HeuQT_N2IbxHKBRkSj8Kcg3fe03LlIsC4cPyYamD27LnKhERg8KDddex2Hmda3Ulh2HmKOn4TZiuHIlfdg6o3NmIIhh37hRi4xlg6elH3nctgLh7f1DLoH97whCdphnsEgZ5LWQl5F0fiSphIrhxJTRq9moBdji-d-CIA2_PJNazBuI2h0c86Vcif_cBXiB0I0uC3vaVWytfb165TVYZJak8z_IdXJAD3jadI-Se1tatTC7qA4SGCNcaCCyiDoiLxc7gfi9Zy-acEdHC18hO-V3ntHaG3SPNxDRKysaT-h0qHfgyJOdJ0Iif6W-8l43h4DNr_fC8D-H0h93z6j1eOfil_nU6YYA_Q2xqVj9u11i8fRGUoa7HS05fzLn4uc_Mnv4n67-toHTnjqPgAJw2xKOsBe2RxvRP5EBFaDSRyyFLO8NSxQADGFVqNgMxbuKTdgRfKKJCKkQSXTRtg2ysYX0-TfaS8Blj8UwnGPtwP98glFNjZESEpY9ccM-3G572MZM3G8d5AA37DsabAJ8LLfm67nOgAS1i5)
**_ **_Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win._**
_
_ _[](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=9fd8f503417e468e9ad434010fbc26f5&_e=Q2QhtOs7iHx__8GaYdG3M1UzBTpdM1S96Y8dog1rn-IPgd0LsHSgtTQ3KOdX1S1D8ZkOv_XxEZk4VRovZxFgMYQY25aMr88j44hu5KHqWRss0xoHMrEOmjx1XQ2a2pvf8_fbWkibtxIPiT61wLb0fhR7_btqVDPBQTH4YYd13_NsuOpRh1v3uny78XRZJT16mt5pPCN1NuUuqakFbNHslBg4iCCUik_-pqlCamRNkxukMz4DSqGkl8U8G4dU4bb2Efb0JHqX3Dmz88lFC7F31XgEgrKqnmGKPwPY0uKU1SUGowMIOUuecDGRrYyKwAMDcQHNYD4CrvWCHP2H9jI1Omx4YrUfMAjXrNn9RrUdNOx4NCIRzdLAytJHOT-AXYPL-Aob4Hy8unWKrxr3XFkzFoXib-DMXsMJW8DdxjRqC7ueTQH08czDh6p52kFC1SAgkk9F1wRycpwwdTmUOLLQzk2wVfsCRUluzzlHca5Vjv4ZzoqQQu1Yh-4eOfId5BH3oBDslkFUdnl8Eqm9oOhiXtIRFbZcgnRKRnVbtSC437vlPLSNvNn8Kh8TK2tSpho0EIEALbYL3hW2kgaOcXJ8UTjMUoDhBkR5IWKon5oK0oscE0_bAIBnpQmoP1zwQWXUVyHLXx6n9I9GHo1XGSGIqihLQQOPPl_kWNilCEZvkGgbszbToDeGkqjG46z_ruvYaaEaPrrsP1AnuuzKFE8auUBtOUS9vy-FgvjfKtE5eRa4DoHmyLGlvwznJ6SDgusLAvSKy2l-zsYYmg47vQWEqHZUXQ_beKsQ6y0ebQdPRg4%3D)_

This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. **But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for.**

And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for.

We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest.

**If you believe in what I’m fighting for,[even $5 goes a long way.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=9fd8f503417e468e9ad434010fbc26f5&_e=Q2QhtOs7iHx__8GaYdG3M1UzBTpdM1S96Y8dog1rn-IPgd0LsHSgtTQ3KOdX1S1DmnW0QWCQLw84c0OUXxazw0PcTjfS1PBQv8Smj1HHi3jY4g5aFh3tV6lY7uni1qIngzaDRuyl0l7bvAML6P1WGyagKGMD2sc57ZvwqorBx2C4XlR3lpBNCQ7dXLpwnfBxOPBQwMMK-gjnh22dqZF9xiAzGH_uoF_NA7UuMAUtJU4t8t37J6bhp6wnu4g1ekrBIlfyu_NZf2p3tps0yeDvpwc-w6Tzd_1HeuQT_N2IbxF8o5haNuzfEcgXIhAC4iKTzd8xn2RUN2rwhgFqkGOzBeWkqQkesPY7z9e_DAQplIVxDJ-7PHEY7VORs0MWwmRgQSm8BMUtXGUpWCV1SvXKz2vlxxGjkvlcRWS90DArOMvrcumHOX5RI04s3IZUEU27rwt4BHN_WmQya0FOi4SJYUdWB5pzu2BkwsvK1gRjLZo6rT_9CCdeOjfsuc56-WPmsXYIqRPBVaZHKCExl5vKEdfscoYVUUsIcLxR0P9CWLh-MCQMHy90GmEIP0Db6x7rIMfJ5OcORm-Qv2xbVXOeTaaX_WHAGKhGGvl6uE80k0NqdGa8VWwGhlWhXzskSHx9z9u_ZHhOykan1AFmWZrozM25MuedvCDn1gyqL5m6eWYuAHru9EW1YELzftaPUu5bQ8XKKfasKEBSuZ3zVGKxc1NWPms8sWICxTghMaUcnnjGI-UPBgqZxH9RVTbiW0cNhEUrIQXyjes9yc4bMeTMYNkYEJubZAfwOgQKiV-iEn6b1V-NauA0XTRbv1dpkkZz)**

clean_body: I’m in this race, not because I want to impress anyone in particular, but because I believe in a life of service, and mine is just getting started. | - Fellow Conservative, I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to. Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss. While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos. Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away. I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— just weeks after 9/11. I didn’t wait. I went. And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map. I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter. Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign. Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win. This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for. And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for. We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest. If you believe in what I’m fighting for, If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now. Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together. You’ve done your part for this country. In liberty Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

I’m a gay, Afro-Latino man and a proud product of the Bronx. I was raised by a single mother who kept my family afloat on a _$4.25 minimum wage._ **I grew up in public housing ridden with mold, leaks, vermin, and without consistent heat or water.**

All the while, the city was shelling out millions on a Trump golf course right across from the street from me – _rather than housing for struggling New Yorkers._

**But Peter, it’s my lived experience that fuels my fight everyday in Washington.**

Peter, I know what it’s like to be left behind by political leaders who pledged to serve you. I know what it’s like to fight with everything you’ve got to make your voice heard. And I know what it’s like to have to hide who you are for fear of violence.

**So in 2020, I ran for Congress to deliver real and meaningful change for the hardworking people of the Bronx – _the people who raised me._**

We’ve achieved so much together, _but there is still so much work to do._

[That’s why I’m reaching out today, Peter: Will you chip in $3 or $7 to support this people-powered movement in our fight to protect my seat, flip the House, and fuel my common-sense, progressive agenda in Congress so I can continue fighting for you for years to come? »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111329008/556864791/-721819307?amount=3&refcode=RTFC_20250711_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NTc4NWZmNi05NzVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yEbBVF10ntECwOVVuyNn2aTPrtQEP2ccosRARUMVANs=)

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

clean_body: | = Hi Peter, I wanted to take a moment to reintroduce myself and explain why public service is so important to me. = | = I’m a gay, Afro-Latino man and a proud product of the Bronx. I was raised by a single mother who kept my family afloat on a $4.25 minimum wage. I grew up in public housing ridden with mold, leaks, vermin, and without consistent heat or water. All the while, the city was shelling out millions on a Trump golf course right across from the street from me – rather than housing for struggling New Yorkers. But Peter, it’s my lived experience that fuels my fight everyday in Washington. Peter, I know what it’s like to be left behind by political leaders who pledged to serve you. I know what it’s like to fight with everything you’ve got to make your voice heard. And I know what it’s like to have to hide who you are for fear of violence. So in 2020, I ran for Congress to deliver real and meaningful change for the hardworking people of the Bronx – the people who raised me. We’ve achieved so much together, but there is still so much work to do. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = Thank you, Ritchie | | Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458 www.torres.nyc Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in. Peter, please, consider making a =. Ohio Republicans — from Columbus to Congress — are pushing an extreme, out-of-touch agenda: gutting public education, attacking reproductive rights, slashing access to healthcare, and undermining our democracy. They’ve made it clear, they’ll do whatever it takes to gain total control, no matter the cost to working families, our kids, or our future.

But here’s the good news: we have a real shot not only to take back Ohio — but to take back Congress, too. And it starts right now, right here at home. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: It is not enough to tell each other what we’re against.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

It is not enough to tell each other what we’re against.

In order to win political power, in order to have a chance for a better
world, we must share what we are for.

We can't only point out who’s screwing you and who is screwing up. We must
have a vision for how we’re going to fix a system that’s failed all but
the most powerful in this country.

So imagine this: A world where the United States does not turn its back on
its allies and friends around the world, but instead works together to
pursue peace and not to start new wars.

And what if, in the future, we really stood for human rights and self
determination?

What if the United States was no longer complicit in the slaughter of tens
of thousands of children and families in Gaza? What if we used our
extraordinary power to convene the countries of the world to ensure there
is a sovereign, independent state for the Palestinian people? And that we
worked for the safety and security of all in that region.

What if we brought that same logic back home? So no one had to fear a
plainclothes federal agent without a badge or without a warrant sweeping
you off the streets, to detain you, to send you to prison for life in El
Salvador.

And what if we followed that by rewriting our immigration laws in our own
image and with our own values? Let’s once and for all legalize Dreamers,
legalize their parents, and legalize every working American that makes
this country great.

And what if in Texas, where it is harder to cast a ballot and register to
vote than any other state in the union, we lived in a real democracy? One
where politicians don’t pick their voters? What if we had online and same
day registration?

And what if we got money out of politics? No more corporations buying
candidates and elections.

Imagine a Texas where teachers didn’t have to work two or three jobs to
make ends meet. Where Greg Abbott didn’t funnel resources from public
schools to private schools with his voucher scheme.

What if we stopped shoveling trillions of dollars to the wealthiest and
most powerful in this country and instead spent that money on things we
really need like building homes, researching and curing cancer, and
combating climate change before it’s too late?

What if we had a Democratic Party that fought for every single one of us
and didn’t roll over or make deals with Trump?

This is the future we must build. But we won’t get there by hoping. We’ll
get there by acting—right now. Just look at the work our Powered by People
volunteers do, registering and then turning out the young voters we need
to win elections in Texas. They’re out there everyday, in election years
and non-election years.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250711.S9UNJC&refcode=ema20250711-240215&t=1001&akid=240215%2E444211%2E82MH3i ]I'm doing everything I can to make this future real. If you're with me,
chip in whatever you can today to help Powered by People register voters,
hold Trump accountable, and win the future we believe in.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250711.S9UNJC&refcode=ema20250711-240215&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=240215%2E444211%2E82MH3i ]DONATE $3

Thank you,

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1021&akid=240215%2E444211%2E82MH3i

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

We’ll get there by acting—right now. Just look at the work our Powered by People volunteers do, registering and then turning out the young voters we need to win elections in Texas. They’re out there everyday, in election years and non-election years. [ ]I'm doing everything I can to make this future real. If you're with me, chip in whatever you can today to help Powered by People register voters, hold Trump accountable, and win the future we believe in. [ ]DONATE $3 Thank you, Beto

name: State Watch (via DLCC.org)
subject: “Alligator Alcatraz” is just the beginning of a horrific GOP plan.
disclaimer_text: paid for by DLCC Victory Fund, 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005
body:
Trump needs his state-level allies. The most direct way to fight back is by defeating them at the ballot box and electing state Democrats who won’t bow to his tirade of authoritarianism. | | |
---
| |
---|---
Several states are considering creating their own _'Alligator Alcatraz'_ facilities. And the Trump administration is urging state GOP leaders to follow suit. _'Alligator Alcatraz'_ is just the beginning of a horrific GOP plan. Officials in Georgia and Arkansas revealed that they already have their own version of these inhumane detention facilities in the works. This situation makes one thing clear: Trump’s regime is looking to the states to do its bidding and is depending on MAGA Republicans up and down the ballot to rubber-stamp their cruelty. [**Before you read any further: We need folks like you to take a stand with us against this dangerous campaign and help elect Democratic leaders who will block the GOP’s assault on our democracy.** ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu05XtZnrBMT5R_mtAuAjxDgicZfgb3kpC3Q-b6VCjsBSlqhECOxd9479o_u6qh365jJW47EAWmsehUKJ03B03FYz4GMiyYtUh2BmX-3vmnJzO2mGdZFBzWK5BcVu68vnHwm9putpCptVR0e7VgjJhIzjmRCErnAk4wXZdME-Fe9z6wWX4wjAMN5GteB-7yvmsvSGyOlypgR6thjIeQZpCDiR0LNpkl9qsyBCkap-YPyDmgSKwWusWyYziUFumkW4I4JxUKCqWr4WxSL4PiS3B3fH2ZIPpQYkyZHWDkLKi5ZNTiX35OgNXyGH9oUhAwX1WYPHnCkVaAnzaig-X1coAS8qpOId3b0OmS204in7Yof0Klw_xYP-iUxiBUuFHivH8_eKfWOWVYLZ4ftzMbVd9rG1X17IUFvSQQ4-rzwQfRA4MDbP91fCuPCq_x-Oa8hXztcI5KNT40-Vnb178Ksp29VCRvGaGz06xfqRVmwpOkvQ/4i3/Fzy1Ll0PSNWzVczAIsaUYQ/h0/h001.FRPrNuhWTssrJSBcWv4XnEJRn2ZKHWpQqJ09qBfGuU0) **[Support state Democrats »](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu05XtZnrBMT5R_mtAuAjxDgicZfgb3kpC3Q-b6VCjsBSlqhECOxd9479o_u6qh365jJW47EAWmsehUKJ03B03FYz4GMiyYtUh2BmX-3vmnJzO2mGdZFBzWK5BcVu68vnHwm9putpCptVR0e7VgjJhIzjmRCErnAk4wXZdME-Fe9z6wWX4wjAMN5GteB-7yvmsscjq8NH2NYrw5KDJ8U59bTCw_jAc-Br2f53qobyrO_LwcOBMX4NXhlJKxg-7QXasHb3blj6KHbYQGwKvhqLhjswmMoDMqKTD0HBrkHOqiJ-tcdxTopl_DDj8iJwaxn7T3WVjvQd06ENn_FdsVg6oJyXHXdZLwmmpQBqBsr2Ad2a1s8ekifUo8dzhRJJ9g9lb94CTERqEA3O6U7vgYGA6hW51mYWdeNXnzODCwluH10eeU4af8_Kq3yBMRV9IZzdiQM4Sje4ZqjmOe3tt5PqT5GlxeROO8OSKIG02Bbz1uwQ/4i3/Fzy1Ll0PSNWzVczAIsaUYQ/h1/h001.tVtpGC_Gi8_uHthe7D42e4Dopgkbfj0eNWX3C4le1P4)** We can’t sit back while these atrocities are being carried out. clean_body: Trump needs his state-level allies. The most direct way to fight back is by defeating them at the ballot box and electing state Democrats who won’t bow to his tirade of authoritarianism. | | | | | ---|--- Several states are considering creating their own 'Alligator Alcatraz' facilities. And the Trump administration is urging state GOP leaders to follow suit. 'Alligator Alcatraz' is just the beginning of a horrific GOP plan. Officials in Georgia and Arkansas revealed that they already have their own version of these inhumane detention facilities in the works. This situation makes one thing clear: Trump’s regime is looking to the states to do its bidding and is depending on MAGA Republicans up and down the ballot to rubber-stamp their cruelty. We can’t sit back while these atrocities are being carried out. Knowing that Trump needs the support of his state-level allies, the most direct way we can fight back is by defeating them at the ballot box and electing state Democrats who won’t bow to his tirade of authoritarianism. Democratic power is the strongest tool we have to combat GOP extremism. The DLCC fights year-round to elect Democrats who will fight to defend our democracy and block the MAGA movement from amassing more power. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | | Together, Team DLCC | Prepared and paid for by DLCC Victory Fund, 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005. name: Voter Research [Voter Action Project]
subject: Voter survey: Trump's tariffs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=jB6WoRqfLRcyaiLuOhpV5Iq4toJhlxoJGYAtdVKUDZFJbVx3skicY1MdZFMs521PR3+L1Iv8rIn/fekVDMFAO/ORiK+JDcF6FSHYo83SwlY=]
AP: Fear that Trump tariffs will spark recession wipes out more than $2 trillion in value from US stocks [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/AP_TrumpTariffMarketRecession.png]
Team: President Donald Trump slapped sweeping tariffs on foreign imports again. Last time he did this, the stock market reacted with major losses as U.S. financial markets closed with their biggest one-day drop since the pandemic flattened the global economy five years ago.
Many economists have said these tariffs are much worse than expected, and investors have dumped shares in companies they predict will suffer most from what is effectively a business tax. In many cases that tax will be passed on to consumers.
“ This is a game changer, not only for the U.S. economy but for the global economy, ” Olu Sonola, Fitch Ratings’ head of U.S. Economic Research, said in a report. “ Many countries will likely end up in a recession. ”
We would like to hear from more voters in your area on this issue. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Team: Are you worried that Trump's tariffs will tank our economy?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=jB6WoRqfLRcyaiLuOhpV5Iq4toJhlxoJGYAtdVKUDZFJbVx3skicY1MdZFMs521PR3+L1Iv8rIn/fekVDMFAO/ORiK+JDcF6FSHYo83SwlY=] NO [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=jB6WoRqfLRcyaiLuOhpV5Iq4toJhlxoJGYAtdVKUDZFJbVx3skicY1MdZFMs521PR3+L1Iv8rIn/fekVDMFAO/ORiK+JDcF6FSHYo83SwlY=]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=jB6WoRqfLRcyaiLuOhpV5Iq4toJhlxoJGYAtdVKUDZFJbVx3skicY1MdZFMs521PR3+L1Iv8rIn/fekVDMFAO/ORiK+JDcF6FSHYo83SwlY=]
