name: Sen. Jacky Rosen
subject: URGENT: Do you approve of the new Trump-GOP budget law?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada Rosen for Nevada
body:
Please respond to my urgent poll before midnight:
| | |
---
Friend,
I grew up in a working-class family as the granddaughter of immigrants and the daughter of a World War II veteran. I worked multiple jobs and took out student loans to pay for college and became the first in my family to graduate.
**The monstrous budget law that President Trump just signed is one of the worst betrayals of hardworking families in history – and we won’t forget it.**
The extreme and cruel Republican budget law will cut almost **_$1 TRILLION_** from Medicaid, making millions of Americans lose their health care coverage. It will cut almost **_$200 BILLION_** from SNAP, which provides food assistance to families who need it the most. And it will kill **_MILLIONS_** of good-paying jobs for hardworking Americans in industries like clean energy.
Families like the one I grew up in will be poorer, sicker, and hungrier because of this new law – all to pay for Trump and his billionaire friends to get even more tax cuts. Just take a look at this chart:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330280/556869086/1253100634?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=37087&ask=5&amounts=250,100,50,25,5&code=jtk5561-su-nd&refcode=jtk5561-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzNjMjI3NC05YjVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KksV2HwW15BapCrD2nNkQFf67WUimP2DvMRXBT_SGE8=&emci=737559dc-de5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=973c2274-9b5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=3800590)
Unlike me, Trump grew up wealthy. His father sent him to private schools and bankrolled his first businesses. He has bankrupted several of these businesses without ever having to fear going hungry or being without health care.
Now, as president, Trump is fighting for the rich and powerful like himself at the risk of the whole rest of the country going bankrupt.
**That's why I am doing everything I can in the Senate to reverse the effects of Trump's disastrous new law – but I need to know Democrats like you are with me. That’s why I’m looking for 1,000 supporters like you to respond to my urgent poll before midnight. [Please, tell me now:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330281/556869087/1253100634?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=37087&ask=5&amounts=250,100,50,25,5&code=jtk5561-su-nd&refcode=jtk5561-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzNjMjI3NC05YjVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KksV2HwW15BapCrD2nNkQFf67WUimP2DvMRXBT_SGE8=&emci=737559dc-de5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=973c2274-9b5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=3800590)**
**Do you approve of the Trump-Republican budget law?**
| | | [YES](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330282/556869088/1253100634?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=37087&ask=5&amounts=250,100,50,25,5&code=jtk5561-su-nd&refcode=jtk5561-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzNjMjI3NC05YjVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KksV2HwW15BapCrD2nNkQFf67WUimP2DvMRXBT_SGE8=&emci=737559dc-de5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=973c2274-9b5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=3800590)
---
| [NO](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330283/556869089/1253100634?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=37087&ask=5&amounts=250,100,50,25,5&code=jtk5561-su-nd&refcode=jtk5561-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzNjMjI3NC05YjVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KksV2HwW15BapCrD2nNkQFf67WUimP2DvMRXBT_SGE8=&emci=737559dc-de5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=973c2274-9b5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=3800590)
---
Thank you for speaking up for working families.

Jacky
|

name: Representative Vikki Breese Iverson
subject: 2025 End of Session Update
body:
The 2025 legislative session has come to a close. Thank you for the opportunity to represent House District 59 and rural Oregon.
---
| | | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcontent.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fbulletins%2F3e7f317/1/01010197fb55dd2f-401b0208-4e79-4339-abe0-b661ebf5a793-000000/-shLZCfTzFvRIpQUAMBxjZ2DIOn-dFc7TcSY2oJW4UY=413)
---|---
---
The 2025 legislative session has come to a close. Thank you for the opportunity to represent House District 59 and rural Oregon. As your state representative, I have sought to bring common sense, accountability, and a strong voice for rural values to your Capitol. This session we saw meaningful victories and painful defeats. Despite the challenges of this session (and there were many), I was able to partner with likeminded legislators to effectively fight back against overreach from the far left, the nonprofit industrial complex and environmental fringe groups. Together, we focused on what matters most to our communities. Too often, decisions in Salem are made without understanding the realities of life East of the Cascades. I’ll continue standing strong, making sure our voices are not just heard but the impact to rural Oregon is considered in legislation coming forward. |
* * *
---
Below is a quick review of key issues that I pushed forward in the 2025 session. I am proud of what we accomplished for rural Oregon!
## **Progress Made**
* **PASSED: SB 83 – Wildfire Map Repeal**
* Possibly the most significant accomplishment this session is the passage of SB 83 to repeal the deeply flawed wildfire maps and their accompanying mandates passed by overwhelming margins. This is a huge victory for rural Oregon. I worked with Republican and Democratic colleagues to repeal these maps. [There is a lot more detail on this issue from the Oregon Property Owners Association](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Foregonpropertyowners.org%2F2025-session-recap-against-the-odds-rural-oregon-fought-and-won%2F/1/01010197fb55dd2f-401b0208-4e79-4339-abe0-b661ebf5a793-000000/5Wc1C-aBi1CeIrgxDOR-LO9JBCREm6yV1NIB7IITTcg=413) who were absolutely critical to drafting and passing this legislation as well.
* **PASSED: SB 777 – Wolf Compensation**
* Wolves aren’t as much of a problem in Portland and Salem, but they significantly harm ranchers in Central and Eastern Oregon. I pushed to adjust compensation to now include a multiplier of three to five times depending on the livestock.
* **PASSED: SB 974 – 120 Day Permit Shot Clock**
* Together with Senator Dick Anderson, the vice chair of Senate Housing committee, we passed a massive consolidation of regulation. Now when housing is being built where housing is planned, builders will have certainty about how long their permits should take.
* **RULEMAKING: HB 3133 – Preserving Family Farm Stands**
* We embraced government efficiency and instead of passing a new law, hope to streamline government regulations of farm stands to help family farms not only stay in business, but be more successful in their communities.
* **FUNDED: HB 3135 – Western Juniper Removal Projects**
* In 2023, local Central and Eastern Oregon soil and water districts identified lands to remove Juniper overgrowth and study the effect on watersheds. I secured funding to ensure we can continue the effort to monitor the effect of Juniper, which are big water wasters, for the full five years.
* **PASSED: HB 3136 – Planning Commission Fairness and Modernization**
* This modernized laws unfairly preventing certain kinds of occupations from serving on planning commissions while preserving local control.
* **PASSED****: HB 3137 – Modernizing Statutes for Real Estate Agents**
* The real estate industry came to the legislature asking for updates to the statutes to protect them and their consumers. As a REALTOR® myself, I appreciated the opportunity to bring this forward.
* **PASSED: HB 3141 – Oregon Farmer and Rancher Day**
* Oregon’s farmers and ranchers don’t go get up early and work late for the recognition, but they deserve to be recognized. I appreciate my fellow legislators who voted to pass this overwhelmingly. We are brainstorming ideas for how to recognize the first annual Oregon Farmer and Rancher Day on March 17, 2026.
* **PASSED: HB 3349 – Rangeland Fire Protection Association Funding and Equipment**
* This wildfire season started close to home. We are all thankful for the Lower Bridge RFPA for protecting so many homes and rangeland from the Alder Springs fire. I always look for opportunities to help the brave volunteer RFPAs across our state and this was a big victory.
* **PASSED: HB 3746 – Condo Construction Liability Reform**
* We need all housing types. Condos don’t often make sense in rural Oregon, but in more urban regions, Condos are a great opportunity for first time buyers. We more fairly reformed liability to hopefully spur some construction which has declined in recent years due to high insurance and litigation costs.
* **WORKGROUP: HB 3928 – Build Oregon Rural Housing Now (BORHN) Act**
* I am excited to continue the conversation about how we protect productive lands and provide the kind of housing folks in rural Oregon want to buy and live in.
* **FUNDED** : As co-chair of the Central Oregon Caucus, I led efforts to fund initiatives important to rural Central Oregon like the CORE3 Public Safety Training Facility, a sewer project in Culver, matching funds for Jefferson County Fire District #1 building expansion and remodel, and an important water and sewer project in our district.

## **Good First Attempts**
* **HB 3139 – Property Tax Protection for Homes Destroyed by Wildfire**
* Oregonians who lose their homes to wildfire shouldn’t be punished with higher property taxes when they rebuild their home.
* **HB 3188 – Mortgage Help for First-Time Homebuyers**
* Reducing costs to first-time home buyers is smart. We need to help more Oregonians become homeowners and I believe that conversation is worth continuing to have.
* **HB 3476 – Funding Improvements for 3 rd Street in Prineville**
* ODOT has made many promises to local governments over the years. When promises aren’t kept, this impacts trust in our agencies. Rural projects like 3rd Street in Prineville are all part of that consideration. I think we will be talking about transportation in the short session (if not before).
* **HB 3572 – Emergency Management Services (EMS) Modernization**
* Rural ambulance services are in crisis. We must make progress on this soon. I appreciate the opportunity to work with Rep. Grayber on this issue. It affects so many Oregonians, especially our rural seniors who need access to EMS services at a moments notice. The 911 call will be answered, but what happens when there isn’t an ambulance to send?
* **HB 3844 – Indexing Estate Tax to Inflation**
* President Trump just made some progress raising the Estate Tax cap at the federal level from $10 million to $15 million. Oregon’s death tax still kicks in at $1 million. I believe it should be raised for everyone.
* We did pass HB 3630 which will apply a natural resource exemption up to $15 million for family natural resource business lands in certain circumstances. This is a positive development. We need to change policies like the Estate Tax when they are driving people to retire in other states.
* **HB 3846 – Ochoco National Forest Pilot Program and HJM 11 & 14 – Urging Congress to Return Control of Federal Lands to Oregon**
* Poor management of our sprawling federal lands is our largest wildfire risk. I started a conversation with Oregon Department of Forestry with my bill this session. We will continue the discussion through the interim. We are hearing positive developments from President Trump’s executive order. My hope is these will filter down to U.S. Forest Service who supervise Oregon’s federal forest land.

### **Challenges with Water**
* We cut way back on astronomical increases in water fees at the Oregon Water Resources Department (OWRD) and are building in additional accountability to have OWRD check back with the Natural Resources budget subcommittee to make sure this agency stays on track for Oregonians.
* SB 1154 is a bill I voted against because I do not think it provides the necessary protections for agriculture and historical water rights. However, the bill was revised down significantly to the point where [environmental and social justice organizations don’t support it anymore](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Foregoncapitalchronicle.com%2F2025%2F06%2F10%2Fenvironment-social-justice-groups-withdraw-support-for-governors-key-groundwater-protection-bill%2F/1/01010197fb55dd2f-401b0208-4e79-4339-abe0-b661ebf5a793-000000/LIS8pjfRGiWTUbJyvZVWK3tqk3j2yxnFNLnKfL6H-08=413). Advocacy from rural water users helped make this bill less devastating.

* * *
---
* * *
---
# Back in the District
Now that session is over, I am back in the district working. One of the fun things I was able to do is a ribbon cutting for the largest transportation project in the City of Prineville history. This new road connects neighborhoods, schools, and creates a great traffic congestion reliever for the city. I secured over $9 million for this project in 2023.
* * *
---
# An Update on Housing Across Oregon
As the Vice Chair of the House Housing committee, my goal has been to spur housing production to ensure that Oregonians have the opportunity to buy a home they can afford. We know this is critical for the wellbeing of families and helps secure our economic future as well.
A lot of work was done in 2023 and 2024 sessions related to housing. One of the big things I was able to push across the finish line was SB 1537 in 2024. This bill created the Housing Accountability and Production Office (HAPO), which just launched. It is a joint effort between the Department of Land Conservation and Development (DLCD) and the Building Codes Division (BCD), to help meet Oregon’s housing production goals. The office will support local governments and housing providers by offering technical assistance, enforcing state housing laws, and coordinating across agencies to remove barriers to building housing.
As we discussed, reform to condo construction liability laws and our 120-day shot clock for building permits both address major pinch points in regulations to prevent housing. I am hopeful the addition of regional housing coordinators will more equitably spread housing dollars across the state.
* * *
---
# Rangeland Fire Protection Associations
* Wildland Firefighter Day was July 2nd to recognize Oregon’s 1,200 volunteer wildland firefighters, the Rangeland Fire Protection Associations (RFPAs).
* As I previously mentioned, [HB 3349](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB3349%3FpubDate=2025-06-25-16-13/1/01010197fb55dd2f-401b0208-4e79-4339-abe0-b661ebf5a793-000000/cwdijH88onU24kjC94oOPJrtQ6CWWE-b9hzhevaO5EI=413) was passed allowing Oregon Department of Forestry (ODF) to directly transfer ownership of depreciated firefighting vehicles and supplies, by sale or donation, to RFPAs. It also establishes the Rangeland Protection Association Fund in the State Treasury and appropriates $1 million out of the General Fund for deposit.
* 17 RFPAs were awarded 2025 Volunteer Fire Capacity grants as well as acquire important new equipment. RFPAs are first responders on the rural landscape. We are seeing they are effective and appreciate that they continue to have a conversation about expanding their critical efforts.
* RFPAs have already responded to over 40 fires so far this year (up 10 from this time in 2024). On June 16th the Lower Bridge RFPA (LBRFPA, established in 2022) experienced their first joint operation with an incident management team on the 3,279 acre Alder Springs Fire. In total, the RFPA responded with all nine pieces of association equipment plus another 20 personal rigs. This saved homes and rangeland.

* * *
---
# Protecting Our Second Amendment Rights
**FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**
**Date:** June 25, 2025****
**RURAL OREGON REPUBLICANS STAND TOGETHER DESPITE ONE PARTY RULE**
**SALEM, Ore.****–** Rural Oregon Republicans are proud to represent the resilient, hardworking people of our region in the Oregon State Legislature. Despite being in the minority, we have consistently delivered meaningful victories for our communities by focusing on principled leadership, strategic collaboration, and unwavering commitment to the values that matter most to rural Oregonians—freedom, economic opportunity, and local control.
Representative Greg Smith (R-Heppner), Representative Rick Lewis (R-Silverton), Representative Shelly Boshart Davis (R-Albany), Representative Vikki Breese Iverson (R-Prineville), Representative Mark Owens (R-Crane), Representative Bobby Levy (R-Echo), Representative Gerald ‘Boomer’ Wright (R-Coos Bay), Representative Jeff Helfrich (R-Hood River), Representative Emily McIntire (R-Eagle Point) made the following statement:
**“Governing in the minority requires ingenuity, determination, and a deep understanding of the issues facing rural Oregon. In the 2025 Legislative Session, we have successfully championed policies that protect our agricultural heritage, repealed wildfire insurance maps, securing wildfire funding while relaxing the tax burden on property owners, streamlining home building, and finalizing long term wolf predation funding. By standing together we will continue to win. Engaging in good-faith negotiations, and standing firm on our core principles, we** ’**ve secured victories that strengthen rural Oregon** ’**s economy and way of life for future generations.”**
However, with Republicans in the minority and Portland Democrats controlling the majority, rural Oregon will suffer some losses. Today, that came in the form of Senate Bill 243, which attempts to weaken the 2nd amendment. Rural Oregon House Republicans remain vigilant on 2nd amendment rights for rural Oregon and are **_excited to announce House Bill 3076 was defeated today_**. HB 3076 would have severely hampered gun dealers in Oregon from selling guns.
**“We may be in the minority, but our impact is undeniable. Together, we will continue to deliver results that give all of rural Oregon—a stronger voice.”**
* * *
---
# Executive Order to Ban Student Cell Phones in Schools
This session the legislature attempted to weigh in on the tricky issue of helping teachers navigate their classrooms with student cell phones. The challenge is to limit distraction while allowing local control from each school board.
The legislature didn’t settle on a workable compromise. [Now the Governor has acted with an executive order](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fdrive.google.com%2Ffile%2Fd%2F1R5kfyMYsA6cg3VQKutUxLTIGVpIV2kgB%2Fview/1/01010197fb55dd2f-401b0208-4e79-4339-abe0-b661ebf5a793-000000/-ohQcIY5SVDdFkBs1d71E77b_HmP7niFNgw-4vcAYmI=413). While I think the intention of this order is good, the devil is always in the details of how it is written. An executive order isn’t always flexible enough to accommodate differing viewpoints on how to solve big issues. My hope is each school board is allowed to handle the issue with at the direction of their local communities, not the Department of Education.
* * *
---
# Capitol Renovation Update
| | The Oregon Pioneer, the gold statue that sits atop our great Capitol building, is getting a partial regilding of its external 23K gold leaf. This is only the fifth time that the statue has been regilded since its initial sculpting in 1938. The work on the Gold Man, another name for the Pioneer, is just another step in the Capitol’s revamp project. We are expecting a full re-opening of the Capitol building this fall. Stay tuned for more updates!
---|---|---
* * *
---
# Transportation
The 2025 legislative session ended Friday, June 27th evening at 11:15 pm, after several weeks of scrambling by Democrats on the transportation committee to raise taxes and fees.
Throughout the session, they put forward gas tax increases from 3 cents all the way to 40 cents! These proposals would all be among the largest tax increases in Oregon history. My district has told me that more expensive gas and higher registration fees are not a priority for them. This speaks volumes to me. Oregonians across the state sent this message and it had a big impact in Salem.
Oregonians do not want this right now. Blaming Republicans for not increasing taxes when Democrats have a supermajority and control of the executive branch doesn’t make sense to me. I am sure there will be more to come on this issue. The Governor has the power to call a special session to try and have this conversation again.
* * *
---
# Your Input Matters
I always appreciate the opportunity to take a break from Salem and spend most of my time in my community during this interim period. Please reach out if there are any issues you would like my assistance.
Sincerely,
Vikki Breese Iverson
State Representative
House District 59
**Office Phone:** 503-986-1459
**Mailing Address:** 900 Court St NE, H-386, Salem, OR 97301
**Email:** [Rep.VikkiBreeseIverson@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Rep.VikkiBreeseIverson@oregonlegislature.gov "Representative Vikki Breese Iverson")
**Website:**[http://www.oregonlegislature.gov/breese-iverson](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/http:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fbreese-iverson/1/01010197fb55dd2f-401b0208-4e79-4339-abe0-b661ebf5a793-000000/MS0byeuiPGWWuxjHpOGuLysj1-7ei2GXSPZDy8GW0lE=413)
* * *

clean_body: The 2025 legislative session has come to a close. Thank you for the opportunity to represent House District 59 and rural Oregon. | | | ---|--- The 2025 legislative session has come to a close. Thank you for the opportunity to represent House District 59 and rural Oregon. As your state representative, I have sought to bring common sense, accountability, and a strong voice for rural values to your Capitol. This session we saw meaningful victories and painful defeats. Despite the challenges of this session (and there were many), I was able to partner with likeminded legislators to effectively fight back against overreach from the far left, the nonprofit industrial complex and environmental fringe groups. Together, we focused on what matters most to our communities. Too often, decisions in Salem are made without understanding the realities of life East of the Cascades. I’ll continue standing strong, making sure our voices are not just heard but the impact to rural Oregon is considered in legislation coming forward. | Below is a quick review of key issues that I pushed forward in the 2025 session. I am proud of what we accomplished for rural Oregon! Progress Made PASSED: SB 83 – Wildfire Map Repeal Possibly the most significant accomplishment this session is the passage of SB 83 to repeal the deeply flawed wildfire maps and their accompanying mandates passed by overwhelming margins. This is a huge victory for rural Oregon. I worked with Republican and Democratic colleagues to repeal these maps. who were absolutely critical to drafting and passing this legislation as well. PASSED: SB 777 – Wolf Compensation Wolves aren’t as much of a problem in Portland and Salem, but they significantly harm ranchers in Central and Eastern Oregon. I pushed to adjust compensation to now include a multiplier of three to five times depending on the livestock. PASSED: SB 974 – 120 Day Permit Shot Clock Together with Senator Dick Anderson, the vice chair of Senate Housing committee, we passed a massive consolidation of regulation. Now when housing is being built where housing is planned, builders will have certainty about how long their permits should take. RULEMAKING: HB 3133 – Preserving Family Farm Stands We embraced government efficiency and instead of passing a new law, hope to streamline government regulations of farm stands to help family farms not only stay in business, but be more successful in their communities. FUNDED: HB 3135 – Western Juniper Removal Projects In 2023, local Central and Eastern Oregon soil and water districts identified lands to remove Juniper overgrowth and study the effect on watersheds. I secured funding to ensure we can continue the effort to monitor the effect of Juniper, which are big water wasters, for the full five years. PASSED: HB 3136 – Planning Commission Fairness and Modernization This modernized laws unfairly preventing certain kinds of occupations from serving on planning commissions while preserving local control. PASSED: HB 3137 – Modernizing Statutes for Real Estate Agents The real estate industry came to the legislature asking for updates to the statutes to protect them and their consumers. As a REALTOR® myself, I appreciated the opportunity to bring this forward. PASSED: HB 3141 – Oregon Farmer and Rancher Day Oregon’s farmers and ranchers don’t go get up early and work late for the recognition, but they deserve to be recognized. I appreciate my fellow legislators who voted to pass this overwhelmingly. We are brainstorming ideas for how to recognize the first annual Oregon Farmer and Rancher Day on March 17, 2026. PASSED: HB 3349 – Rangeland Fire Protection Association Funding and Equipment This wildfire season started close to home. We are all thankful for the Lower Bridge RFPA for protecting so many homes and rangeland from the Alder Springs fire. I always look for opportunities to help the brave volunteer RFPAs across our state and this was a big victory. PASSED: HB 3746 – Condo Construction Liability Reform We need all housing types. Condos don’t often make sense in rural Oregon, but in more urban regions, Condos are a great opportunity for first time buyers. We more fairly reformed liability to hopefully spur some construction which has declined in recent years due to high insurance and litigation costs. WORKGROUP: HB 3928 – Build Oregon Rural Housing Now (BORHN) Act I am excited to continue the conversation about how we protect productive lands and provide the kind of housing folks in rural Oregon want to buy and live in. FUNDED : As co-chair of the Central Oregon Caucus, I led efforts to fund initiatives important to rural Central Oregon like the CORE3 Public Safety Training Facility, a sewer project in Culver, matching funds for Jefferson County Fire District #1 building expansion and remodel, and an important water and sewer project in our district. Good First Attempts HB 3139 – Property Tax Protection for Homes Destroyed by Wildfire Oregonians who lose their homes to wildfire shouldn’t be punished with higher property taxes when they rebuild their home. HB 3188 – Mortgage Help for First-Time Homebuyers Reducing costs to first-time home buyers is smart. We need to help more Oregonians become homeowners and I believe that conversation is worth continuing to have. HB 3476 – Funding Improvements for 3 rd Street in Prineville ODOT has made many promises to local governments over the years. When promises aren’t kept, this impacts trust in our agencies. Rural projects like 3rd Street in Prineville are all part of that consideration. I think we will be talking about transportation in the short session (if not before). HB 3572 – Emergency Management Services (EMS) Modernization Rural ambulance services are in crisis. We must make progress on this soon. I appreciate the opportunity to work with Rep. Grayber on this issue. It affects so many Oregonians, especially our rural seniors who need access to EMS services at a moments notice. The 911 call will be answered, but what happens when there isn’t an ambulance to send? HB 3844 – Indexing Estate Tax to Inflation President Trump just made some progress raising the Estate Tax cap at the federal level from $10 million to $15 million. Oregon’s death tax still kicks in at $1 million. I believe it should be raised for everyone. We did pass HB 3630 which will apply a natural resource exemption up to $15 million for family natural resource business lands in certain circumstances. This is a positive development. We need to change policies like the Estate Tax when they are driving people to retire in other states. HB 3846 – Ochoco National Forest Pilot Program and HJM 11 & 14 – Urging Congress to Return Control of Federal Lands to Oregon Poor management of our sprawling federal lands is our largest wildfire risk. I started a conversation with Oregon Department of Forestry with my bill this session. We will continue the discussion through the interim. We are hearing positive developments from President Trump’s executive order. My hope is these will filter down to U.S. Forest Service who supervise Oregon’s federal forest land. Challenges with Water We cut way back on astronomical increases in water fees at the Oregon Water Resources Department (OWRD) and are building in additional accountability to have OWRD check back with the Natural Resources budget subcommittee to make sure this agency stays on track for Oregonians. Summer is here, and so is one of the most critical moments of our year.

We’re officially launching our Summer Membership Drive to power the next generation of Democratic women leaders. But right now, we’re still 412 members short of our goal.

That’s a problem. Especially because July is one of the hardest months to fundraise, and your support now could determine how many women we’re able to train this year.

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Tell Congress: Stop criminalizing homelessness.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Add your name to support the Housing Not Handcuffs Act.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250711-5235-footer&t=1&akid=5235%2E420207%2EEstemS)
Hi Peter,
_Poverty should not be a crime._ But some politicians are trying to make it one.
The Supreme Court just ruled in _Grants Pass v. Johnson_ that cities can fine or jail people simply for sleeping outside, _even when they have nowhere else to go._ This decision is cruel, dangerous, and deeply out of step with our values.
That’s why I just introduced new legislation with Rep. Maxwell Frost to block the federal government from funding efforts to criminalize homelessness. Our bill also puts real solutions like housing and mental health services back at the center of our response to poverty.
Instead of investing in housing, care, and services, Republicans are trying to punish people for being poor. That’s not just morally bankrupt. **It’s a recipe for more suffering.**
Our bill would change that, and we need your voice behind it.
👉 [**Will you sign on to support our new legislation to stop criminalizing homelessness?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/pj-em-20250711-1-housingnothandcuffs?source=em20250711-5235&akid=5235%2E420207%2EEstemS)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/pj-em-20250711-1-housingnothandcuffs?source=em20250711-5235&akid=5235%2E420207%2EEstemS)**
**We can’t let the GOP turn homelessness into a crime.** This is our moment to fight back with compassion and uphold the dignity of every human being.
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20250711-5235-footer&t=2&refcode2=5235_420207_EstemS&akid=5235%2E420207%2EEstemS)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=3&akid=5235%2E420207%2EEstemS)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=5235%2E420207%2EEstemS)

Last month, the Supreme Court ruled that states can block Medicaid funding from going to Planned Parenthood, even though federal law **_already bans taxpayer money from funding abortion._**

Let’s be clear, **_this is about stripping healthcare from women_** : cancer screenings, birth control, pap smears, STI testing, all of it now under attack.

They’re doing it, piece by piece. Through court rulings. Through funding cuts. Through cruelty that targets the most vulnerable people in this country.

And they’re not stopping here. Trump’s allies in Congress passed a national bill that bans Medicaid funding for Planned Parenthood in every single state. **That bill could force hundreds of clinics to close — many in places where abortion is still legal.**

It’s not hypothetical. It’s not a slippery slope. It’s happening right now. And this is why we must win back control of Congress — it is the only way to stop them.

**_[So, Peter, will you chip in $10 or anything you can to help us fight back against Trump’s plan to destroy reproductive care in America?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330595/556870319/1420690541?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_SM_20250711_FR_SCOTUS_0_6MO_Seth_NA_NA_Medicaid&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMjM1Y2U0Ni05ZDVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2XGVokBnWPdhB4X-PaBeGVLv-jBTkcT_TOel0wlKAPY=)_** | | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330596/556870320/1420690541?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_SM_20250711_FR_SCOTUS_0_6MO_Seth_NA_NA_Medicaid&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMjM1Y2U0Ni05ZDVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2XGVokBnWPdhB4X-PaBeGVLv-jBTkcT_TOel0wlKAPY=)
---
Because if we don’t, they win.

Thank you,

Seth

name: Brent Hennrich
subject: Seven counties. 7,000 square miles. One people-powered campaign.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
| | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMU_BeZcmRBr16dPjlrMKhhjiC51ataw3Peft1wIO87ITMjfwYfP9uf8WP-kn8cTDof-319N-mE6LhiNBcwc2ipV5kgeoS6H9KkpXGZU7NF23B5qnezDK8IkcZb1kt8l7mXBg2mKeXFGkknlHBdRkNyBX9qTkWIQx3qRbTceQ4NlLrjgNES9GXreoD7PEhkAT3KFV-52ddeiJ_8ozX3tKXB7ygCNnY7EGbzg_coVlB6RcatNwksIHm2xtRNlS0lIIpC42UhGhmOjCe8rs7BTMNDc/4i3/qZtBzQcOSAu-AUwlvpEWqQ/h0/h001.EB9NKoUVP5sC1tLkc4ptfqgFlf251grw6aG15a7mtKI)
---
Peter,

Washington's 3rd Congressional district is big. We're talking seven counties and over 7,000 square miles. **And if we want to win, we 've got to show up everywhere**. That means early mornings and long drives. It means shaking hands at farmers markets in the morning and sitting down with union members hundreds of miles away that same night. It means printing thousands of pieces of campaign literature, putting gas in the tank, and investing in the tools we need to reach every voter we can. [**That 's why I'm asking you today to become a monthly donor.**](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUDflG8Fa2KaGh3zQ2mDvgYHdzumCO9S7rywADWCz-MzxvrZZ4NyR57rB9CehaGzvoUt2l430JSIUQT1f4oTUBr9MgWZ4Fh8mg_VVfeAXbTJEmq7kumBo54R41hb_2Hw3mdLCYIadt222P9ePjbStww76vy-Y-u9ONPjo2-uSkb-GMBVrwwX5sdHnK1px7OlCTNUAOELWNDpPUeNt6df-AEUgyP6j_bucLSDHjaS-ujcqhs6r8klGZz873JynluxjDth5enFRu3SQXRvXqelKN1nYndVn6KrhXrrQnZwgFfk5mzKuYlQEhY3sbj3JoO5lfDJ3S0sqFsN6Nm-CEBcx3VjRly93bOjk-RY7Wszf0-_jw/4i3/qZtBzQcOSAu-AUwlvpEWqQ/h1/h001.l9MXBSLnff_-ALaWNc7vmTTrFq9M1GNp_U5tP5Oq9CU) Monthly recurring donations help us build a stable campaign budget we can count on. They take the guesswork out of fundraising and let us plan beyond the week-to-week grind of call time. When we have a baseline of monthly donors and know what's coming in each month, we can make smart, strategic decisions. We can hire staff, pay for outreach materials, and keep our organizing team on the road without second-guessing the next stop. And because we are a grassroots movement powered by you, we believe you deserve to know exactly where your money goes. So let me break it down: [**$25 a month**](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUDflG8Fa2KaGh3zQ2mDvgYHE_B6FP4ReR1m5WaunO3UON0xE2V8y9c-WYD1Kla2NcrzvpJo228mN3FnVpXF6QiNMiIA8yqSs7KPXDLcKW8VF5oi0ntRUHI38AwMI0EJ6E30SYDGfUSGp2fuW3zOeCcdw4a6Ss-p2jg5QMPu886FGz4DNX-vKw_lchV2J8WGckBeki34kAVNpDTr5O_UWgLF89Yk01o_ut2aI0kRam5PiVFIk0EINhM1Ts8EFm57PRW_fzxO30uERpAP9xYDXuGLw6ooIgOxQOaD0hPPvkURHSjf_oEZX_4Bv5KPRk9dDzChjCKxFYfTevxiN6sIucgms2cMAfVvw2cIFbcIrg-vbA/4i3/qZtBzQcOSAu-AUwlvpEWqQ/h2/h001.bPgoxgWG9fB6qWEu4G6apEYyTj3jJY1gLG8t53YoT28) helps us print over 1,000 campaign palm cards to hand out at county fairs, union halls, and doorsteps across the county. clean_body: | | Peter, Washington's 3rd Congressional district is big. We're talking seven counties and over 7,000 square miles. And if we want to win, we 've got to show up everywhere. That means early mornings and long drives. It means shaking hands at farmers markets in the morning and sitting down with union members hundreds of miles away that same night. It means printing thousands of pieces of campaign literature, putting gas in the tank, and investing in the tools we need to reach every voter we can. Monthly recurring donations help us build a stable campaign budget we can count on. They take the guesswork out of fundraising and let us plan beyond the week-to-week grind of call time. When we have a baseline of monthly donors and know what's coming in each month, we can make smart, strategic decisions. We can hire staff, pay for outreach materials, and keep our organizing team on the road without second-guessing the next stop. And because we are a grassroots movement powered by you, we believe you deserve to know exactly where your money goes. So let me break it down: helps us print over 1,000 campaign palm cards to hand out at county fairs, union halls, and doorsteps across the county. can buy ad space in local newspapers or pay for permits and table fees at community events. keeps our team moving across the district with gas in the tank and food on the road. would let us hire more field staff and reach every corner of this massive district. And for every new monthly donor we add, we get just a little bit stronger, more stable, and more equipped to fight back against the hedge fund cash and special interest donors propping up my opponent's campaign. If you believe in this movement and want to help us win, become a monthly donor today. We'll put every dollar to work building a movement that puts working families first. Let's Heal Washington! Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. _[If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330870/556871416/1416943042?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535)_ [_**If you'd like to donate to the INDems' Reorganization Fund, you can do so by clicking here.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330871/556871417/1764064610?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=) ****
_LAST CALL FOR ROOM BLOCK: Join us in French Lick for IDEA 2025!_
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330872/556871418/-398826277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=)** This is another reminder about our annual gathering in French Lick before **[our room block closes on July 15!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330873/556871419/-881237850?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) **Please reserve your room today if you plan on staying with us. Hoosier Democrats, we are just over a month away from the 144th annual IDEA Conference in French Lick! This year it will take place [from August 14-17.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330874/556871420/563597392?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) This is one of the longest yearly traditions not just in Indiana, but in all of American politics. Democrats will gather in French Lick and West Baden Springs from all over Indiana to learn from each other, attend Party meetings, and gear up for 2026 and beyond. We will be charging a [small registration fee](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330875/556871421/-398826277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=) of $20 this year for attendees to help fund our programs and training sessions throughout the weekend. This is your invitation to join us for this year’s festivities! Make sure to book a room under our [room block](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330876/556871422/-881237850?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) to ensure you get the best price possible. Also, be sure to [purchase tickets and register for the golf outing](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330877/556871423/-398826277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=), and the Saturday breakfast if you are attending. Non-golfers are welcome to purchase a ticket for the pre-Golf breakfast on Friday. This year promises to be another exceptional French Lick, and we will see you on the porch next month! ****_The Big Ugly Betrayal Threatens the Future of HIP 2.0_
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330878/556871424/946139294?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535)**[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330879/556871425/946139294?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) Indiana could lose hundreds of millions in provider fees and billions in federal Medicaid funding. The new Big Ugly Bill means that Indiana will see $23 billion less in Medicaid funding over the next decade – a number the state will be unable to replace. HIP 2.0 is at risk: enrollment caps, funding shortfalls, work requirements could topple a program that keeps ~750K Hoosiers insured. Coverage disruption likely leads to public health setbacks, deeper rural hospital strain, and loss of services—especially in mental-health and substance-abuse services. Every Indiana Republican voted for this legislation to kick hundreds of thousands off their coverage, and plunge our state into financial peril. ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330880/556871426/1326295384?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535)**
[Share State Sen. Rodney Pol](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330881/556871427/1326295384?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDU3ZDdjMy05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DJlfvYHhnTFMkoWtnnl9RgomeAowdtFj0L9Nyx8rTEQ=&emci=55cf0828-9d5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b057d7c3-9e5e-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) State Senator Rodney Pol (D-Chesterton) has been fighting for years to bring water safety legislation to the table in the state legislature. His efforts have led to more life rings, and life-saving devices being available at beaches across Indiana. After a series of tragic accidents on Lake Michigan this 4th of July weekend, he is once-again advocating for stronger safety legislation. On that front, Sen. Pol and Statehouse Democrats have secured water safety as a topic for the summer study sessions about to take place over the coming months. There is more we can do to make our world-class beaches safe for all Hoosier families. _**People’s Town Hall Series**_ We have more upcoming People’s Town Hall events! clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. = ** LAST CALL FOR ROOM BLOCK: Join us in French Lick for IDEA 2025! = This is another reminder about our annual gathering in French Lick before Please reserve your room today if you plan on staying with us. Hoosier Democrats, we are just over a month away from the 144th annual IDEA Conference in French Lick! This year it will take place This is one of the longest yearly traditions not just in Indiana, but in all of American politics. Democrats will gather in French Lick and West Baden Springs from all over Indiana to learn from each other, attend Party meetings, and gear up for 2026 and beyond. We will be charging a = of $20 this year for attendees to help fund our programs and training sessions throughout the weekend. This is your invitation to join us for this year’s festivities! Make sure to book a room under our to ensure you get the best price possible. Also, be sure to =, and the Saturday breakfast if you are attending. Non-golfers are welcome to purchase a ticket for the pre-Golf breakfast on Friday. This year promises to be another exceptional French Lick, and we will see you on the porch next month! The Big Ugly Betrayal Threatens the Future of HIP 2.0 Indiana could lose hundreds of millions in provider fees and billions in federal Medicaid funding. The new Big Ugly Bill means that Indiana will see $23 billion less in Medicaid funding over the next decade – a number the state will be unable to replace. HIP 2.0 is at risk: enrollment caps, funding shortfalls, work requirements could topple a program that keeps ~750K Hoosiers insured. Coverage disruption likely leads to public health setbacks, deeper rural hospital strain, and loss of services—especially in mental-health and substance-abuse services. Every Indiana Republican voted for this legislation to kick hundreds of thousands off their coverage, and plunge our state into financial peril. Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. State Senator Rodney Pol (D-Chesterton) has been fighting for years to bring water safety legislation to the table in the state legislature. His efforts have led to more life rings, and life-saving devices being available at beaches across Indiana. After a series of tragic accidents on Lake Michigan this 4th of July weekend, he is once-again advocating for stronger safety legislation. On that front, Sen. Pol and Statehouse Democrats have secured water safety as a topic for the summer study sessions about to take place over the coming months. There is more we can do to make our world-class beaches safe for all Hoosier families. People’s Town Hall Series We have more upcoming People’s Town Hall events! Richmond – Thursday, July 24, 2025 at 6:00-7:30 PM ET Allen County Public Library Theater, 900 South A St, Richmond, IN 47374 Information on County Party events, Town Halls, protests, volunteer opportunities, and more can be found on our Make Your Voice Heard! Submit Public Input on Upcoming Indiana Projects INDIANAPOLIS PUBLIC SCHOOLS – IPS is taking comments about the recent legislative session, and the creation of the Indianapolis Local Education Alliance by lawmakers. IN DEPT OF ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT — IDEM is seeking public comment on current environment regulations. This is to implement Braun’s executive order this spring to weaken regulations. ** Upcoming Candidate Trainings the National Democratic Training Committee on Monday, July 21st from 1-2 pm ET for a free virtual live training on Phone Banking for Political Campaigns. name: Dan Goldman
subject: This GOP budget bill is a blatant attack on the American people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, Republicans’ Big Ugly Bill is officially law — in large part because New York’s 7 House Republicans sold out New Yorkers to bow down to Donald Trump. **Their budget is a _blatant attack_ on the overwhelming majority of Americans who are struggling to make ends meet.** New York Republicans are betraying all New Yorkers to placate Donald Trump's billionaire-first agenda. It’s absolutely devastating to see my colleagues betray the very people who got them elected. Take **Rep. Mike Lawler** (NY-17). He assured his constituents that he would not cut Medicaid, yet he voted to do just that and strip health insurance from **32,216** individuals in his district. Similarly, **Elise Stefanik** (NY-21) cast her vote in favor of the GOP BUB, resulting in **32,353** of her constituents losing their health coverage. This will burden our state with _billions of dollars_ in additional costs — and yet she even wants to run the state! We’re seeing a direct consequence of prioritizing partisan agendas above the real needs of the people who trusted these representatives with their votes. It’s a stark reminder of the lengths some will go to serve their own political interests over public good. As your representative, I remain committed to advocating for policies that truly reflect our shared values and prioritize the health and security of all citizens, no matter their political affiliation. I vow to continue holding up my end — standing with you all, fighting for the interests of New Yorkers, and ensuring that our elected officials are held accountable for their actions. **Together, we can keep building a brighter future that upholds the promise of opportunity and justice for everyone.[Please chip in to help us make this future a reality for all New Yorkers.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330862/556871379/534100113?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250711_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNTFiYjM5ZS05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jU9RulctOh9D5zZqpEzqRcKHb8ALLDTAehPoN9jqBiA=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111330863/556871380/534100113?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250711_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNTFiYjM5ZS05ZTVlLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jU9RulctOh9D5zZqpEzqRcKHb8ALLDTAehPoN9jqBiA=)

I vow to continue holding up my end — standing with you all, fighting for the interests of New Yorkers, and ensuring that our elected officials are held accountable for their actions. Together, we can keep building a brighter future that upholds the promise of opportunity and justice for everyone.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you for your continued support and dedication to our cause. We must keep pushing forward and demand better from those in power. # [Trump grieves "young angels" who died in horrific Texas Flood](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=168094772&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0NzcyLCJpYXQiOjE3NTIyNzE2OTQsImV4cCI6MTc1NDg2MzY5NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMwNzI3ODAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-29s5XZfsBaL76y51dmWqE93QtM5CaMmFkVajssulWw)
### Melania is given a heartbreaking gift from flood survivors - This will give you chills...
| | [Politibrawl](https://substack.com/@politibrawl)

| Jul 11

| [](https://substack.com/@politibrawl)

| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=168094772&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDk0NzcyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjI3MTY5NCwiZXhwIjoxNzU0ODYzNjk0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzA3Mjc4MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ACLmj9rTB74SjJzchXmxVWMmK6NiAkj3KcMkIhzM9SU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

President Donald Trump toured Texas flood damage on Friday, **[expressing grief over the "young angels" who died](https://substack.com/redirect/2b010977-f17d-45a2-bcf6-83e6f8f09a2c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at Camp Mystic, a Christian summer camp for girls that he described as "legendary" and one that "people would want to go to from all over the country."
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6ed2c550-f0ae-493e-8c83-e0408736f57e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **The flooding claimed at least 120 lives with 160 others missing** , caused by rapid water-level rise near San Antonio and Austin that Trump compared to "a giant, giant wave in the Pacific Ocean" that even "the best surfers in the world would be afraid to surf."
* **First Lady Melania Trump received a silver charm bracelet** from camp survivors to commemorate the children who died, after having an emotional private meeting where she "prayed with them, hugged, held hands" and heard their stories.
"Our nation is grieving with you. We just met with the wonderful families," the first lady told attendees of the meeting. "We prayed with them, we hugged, we held hands. They shared the stories. And I met beautiful young ladies. They gave me this special bracelet from the camp in honor of all of the little girls that lost their lives."
* **TV personality Dr. Phil McGraw joined the trip** and offered consolation advice, telling people not to avoid mentioning the loss to grieving families, saying "You don't ever let them think you've moved on from it" when encountering bereaved parents or grandparents.

* * *
**[Together with Goldco](https://substack.com/redirect/16646354-9bd0-42b1-8a37-cdff0f94040c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **Trump 's 2025 Economic Revolution Is Here - Are Your Retirement Savings Ready?**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/16646354-9bd0-42b1-8a37-cdff0f94040c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**President Trump 's "One Big Beautiful Bill" strives to deliver the largest tax cuts for working and middle class Americans in our country's history.** His bold plan not only extends the historic 2017 tax cuts but introduces even deeper reforms aimed at empowering American workers and retirees.
If you are retired or near retirement, this could mean more money in your savings and a once-in-a-generation opportunity to help protect lasting wealth--**if you 're prepared**.
**But here 's the catch:**
This new economic agenda may come with both massive opportunity and serious risk for anyone with a traditional retirement account--like a 401(k), IRA, or TSP.
The fatal flaw? These accounts are built on a Wall Street system that assumes stability, free from manipulation and political shocks.
And in today's world, all it could take is one major event--another bank failure, a geopolitical crisis, a market crash--to wipe out decades of your savings like it did in the 2008 Financial Crisis.
**If you have a retirement account and want to help safeguard --not just survive--during Trump's economic revolution, this is your wake-up call.**
We've outlined a wealth protection strategy inside the **[2025 Retirement Protection Guide](https://substack.com/redirect/16646354-9bd0-42b1-8a37-cdff0f94040c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** -- and it's completely FREE.
**Inside this guide, you'll discover an IRS-approved loophole that allows you to move your retirement savings tax-free and penalty-free into a safe haven asset and hedge your wealth against inflation, market volatility and geopolitical crises.**
**Time is short.** Trump's reforms may be coming fast--and the financial system roller coaster may be a ride that you don't want to take with your retirement savings.

* * *
# Democrat Senator SPEAKS OUT against socialist NYC mayoral candidate
**[Democrat Sen. John Fetterman is speaking out](https://substack.com/redirect/0dc57b25-e848-4025-be6b-712bf4615b95?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** against NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in recent statements condemning the socialist's views.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/23186c5f-b34f-45d0-bfa5-ecaf6f625b3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Fetterman questions Mamdani 's place in Democratic Party** - Fetterman (D-Pa.) criticized Mamdani on Fox News: "He's not even really a Democrat, honestly," and adding, "I don't really agree with virtually any of it politically."
_" He is a socialist, a proud socialist. He's not a technical Democrat. I'm close friends with many very, very left Democrats in the Senate, but again we don't have those kinds of views,"_ Fetterman emphasized in a **[Friday FOX News segment.](https://substack.com/redirect/23186c5f-b34f-45d0-bfa5-ecaf6f625b3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* **Mamdani divides Democrats amid NYC mayoral bid** - While many Democratic moderates rallied behind Mamdani after his primary win, others have faced backlash for not offering support. Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand (D-N.Y.) apologized after previously referencing "global jihad" in connection to Mamdani.
* **Controversy over Mamdani 's rhetoric and ideology** - Mamdani has declined to denounce the slogan "globalize the intifada," a chant linked to violent resistance against Israel. Despite this, Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer also went online to call demands for Mamdani's deportation "disgusting" and praised his campaign on social media.
A self-described democratic socialist and son of Columbia University professor and film director, Mamdani supports policies like state-run grocery stores, free buses, and public housing.

* * *
# Largest teachers' union misspells 'fascism' in anti-Trump statement
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c63bf328-e802-45ac-8b93-04271f80e1ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**[Social media responded](https://substack.com/redirect/fd4e8089-fc5b-43d8-9af8-8c439c22e680?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to the National Education Association, the nation's largest teacher's union, misspelling "fascism" in a recent anti-Trump statement.
* **NEA mocked for spelling error** - The NEA drew widespread mockery this week after it misspelled "fascism" as "facism" in a resolution condemning President Donald Trump, approved Sunday at its annual convention.
* **Union vows to 'defend democracy' against Trump** - The resolution pledged NEA resources to oppose Trump's "program and actions," calling it a defense of "the survival of civilization itself." The agenda item, posted online, declared the NEA would use the wrongly-spelled term "'facism'" to characterize Trump's policies.
* **Critics highlight irony and politicization** - "Yes, the union that claims to represent educators couldn't even spell 'fascism' correctly in its official resolution attacking the president," Corey A. DeAngelis wrote in a Fox News Digital op-ed. Others on social media quickly joined in, calling the move both partisan and embarrassing.
* **Pushback over union 's political agenda** - Figures like Rep. Jim Walsh (R-Wash.) and Moms for Liberty co-founder Tina Descovich criticized the NEA's priorities, while Americans for Fair Treatment CEO Chip Rogers argued, "No teacher should have their money taken to fund hyper-partisan political activities."

* * *
# NEW amendment to ban taxpayer funds for foreign adversary research
**[A pair of new amendments](https://substack.com/redirect/1455b4d2-bbd1-441c-ab90-9feac27d9be8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **in a key military spending bill seek to block U.S. taxpayer dollars from funding research tied to foreign adversaries, including China and Russia in a move led by Sen. Joni Ernst amid mounting scrutiny over past Pentagon spending.
* **Ernst (R-Iowa) targets foreign-linked research and labs** - The amendments, added to the National Defense Authorization Act, would ban Department of Defense funds from supporting academic institutions and research facilities affiliated with the People's Republic of China or the Russian Federation.
* **Animal testing sites included in crackdown** - A second provision would bar defense spending on animal research at facilities located in or controlled by foreign adversaries, with exceptions only if the Defense Secretary determines it is in the national security interest and informs Congress.
* **Ernst slams past Pentagon spending as 'insane'** - "It is insane that I've had to fight for years to stop funding foreign adversaries' labs and shady experiments by Chinese scientists with American defense dollars," Ernst said. She called the move a win for "common sense, taxpayers, and the safety of our citizens."
* **Audits expose millions funneled to overseas labs** - A 2024 Pentagon audit found over $15.5 million routed to foreign labs for pathogen research -- some confirmed as gain-of-function. Ernst warned, "Washington hasn't learned any lessons from COVID-19."

If passed, the amendments would take effect during fiscal year 2026.

| Hi Peter, I wanted to send a quick note ahead of the weekend.

As the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue in 2025, I feel the pressure to _win big_ at the ballot box. Experts are calling this race a toss-up and our opponents are launching new attacks every day, so I'm asking my strongest supporters for a small contribution.
